3. forwarded instruction reaches an external program, plugin, adapter, or later CPI hop with signer privilege unexpectedly preserved on an authority/user/PDA-adjacent account.
4. external program takes a branch that should have been impossible after signer stripping: initialize, approve, mutate, withdraw, delegate, or reconfigure.
5. team misdiagnoses the incident because the call site visibly asked to disable signer propagation, so reviewers trust the abstraction instead of inspecting the serialized `AccountMeta` vector.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A70** = callerк∞А signer privilegeл•Љ мЛ§мИШл°Ь нШємЭА лђімЛђмљФ мЩЄлґА CPIмЧР мІБм†С м†ДлЛђнХШлКФ нМ®нДімЭілЛ§.
- **A117** = callerк∞А **лґДл™ЕнЮИ signerл•Љ м†Ьк±∞нХШл†§к≥† мЛЬлПДнЦИлКФлН∞**, framework-generated serialization к≤љк≥ДмЧРмДЬ кЈЄ downgradeк∞А м§См≤© к≥Дм†ХмЧР л∞ШмШБлРШмІА мХКлКФ нМ®нДімЭілЛ§. м¶Й "кґМнХЬ м†ДлЛђ" мЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **кґМнХЬ м†Ьк±∞ мЛ§нМ®** к∞А нХµмЛђмЭілЛ§.
- **A113** = Token-2022 CPI Guard owner pubkey confusion к∞ЩмЭА owner-field wiring лђЄм†Ь.
- **A117** = owner к∞ТмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **serialized `AccountMeta.is_signer` provenance** лђЄм†ЬлЛ§.
- **A112** = IDL / tooling metadata trust-boundary confusion.
- **A117** = generated client/account-meta emission pathмЭШ **runtime privilege-shaping bug** лЛ§.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. к∞РмВђмЮРлКФ л≥інЖµ on-chain instruction logicл•Љ л≥імІА, generated `to_account_metas` code pathкєМмІА лВіл†§к∞АмІАлКФ мХКлКФлЛ§.
2. нПЙнПЙнХЬ(flat) к≥Дм†Х кµђм°∞мЧРмДЬлКФ overrideк∞А м†ХмГБ лПЩмЮСнХ† мИШ мЮИмЦі, **composite/nested accounts** мЧРмДЬлІМ лУЬлЯђлВШлКФ нЪМкЈАл•Љ лЖУмєШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.
3. л¶ђлЈ∞мЦілКФ `Some(false)` к∞ЩмЭА нШЄмґЬ нЭФм†БмЭД л≥іл©і мЭілѓЄ signer м†Ьк±∞к∞А мЩДл£МлРРлЛ§к≥† мЛђл¶ђм†БмЬЉл°Ь к≤∞л°†лВіл¶ђкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.
4. нЕМмК§нКЄк∞А honest target + direct accounts м°∞нХ©мЧР мєШмЪ∞мєШл©і, proxy/remaining-accounts forwarding к≤љк≥ДмЭШ privilege leakл•Љ мЮђнШДнХШмІА л™їнХЬлЛ§.

**PoC scenario (future Solana adapter pattern)**:
```rust
// team believes signer privilege is stripped before forwarding
let mut metas = external::client::accounts::Init {
    authority,
    vault,
    system_program,
}.to_account_metas(Some(false));

// old generated path may preserve signer on nested/composite fields
invoke(&Instruction { program_id: external_program, accounts: metas, data }, infos)?;

// external program unexpectedly still sees authority as signer
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- Anchorл•Љ `#3322` fix нПђнХ® л≤Дм†ДмЬЉл°Ь мШђл¶і к≤Г
- external CPI / proxy / adapter к≤љл°ЬмЧРмДЬлКФ "нШЄмґЬлґАмЧРмДЬ `Some(false)` л•Љ м§ђлЛ§" л•Љ м¶Эк±∞л°Ь мВЉмІА лІРк≥†, **мµЬмҐЕ serialized `AccountMeta` list** мЧР unexpected signerк∞А мЧЖлКФмІА лЛ®мЬД нЕМмК§нКЄл°Ь к≤Ам¶ЭнХ† к≤Г
- composite account struct / nested accounts / remaining-accounts forwardingмЭі мЮИлКФ к≤љл°ЬлКФ signer downgrade regression testл•Љ л≥ДлПДл°Ь лСШ к≤Г
- privilege downgradingмЭі м§СмЪФнХЬ к≤љл°ЬмЧРмДЬлКФ generated helperл•Љ лІємЛ†нХШмІА лІРк≥†, нХДмЪФнХЬ к≤љмЪ∞ `AccountMeta` л•Љ мИШлПЩмЬЉл°Ь кµђмД±нХ† к≤Г
- мЩЄлґА нФДл°ЬкЈЄлЮ®мЧР лДШкЄ∞лКФ authority/user/PDA к≥Дм†ХмЭА signer мЬ†мІАк∞А мЭШлПДлРЬ к≤љмЪ∞лІМ allowlist-styleл°Ь нЧИмЪ©нХ† к≤Г

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/Cargo.toml` лКФ `anchor-lang = "0.31.1"`, `anchor-spl = "0.31.1"` л•Љ мВђмЪ©нХШк≥†, `keeper/Cargo.toml` лКФ `anchor-client = "0.31.1"` л•Љ мВђмЪ©нХЬлЛ§.
- кЈЄлЯђлВШ нШДмЮђ мК§мЇФнХЬ `programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` / `keeper/src/` к≤љл°ЬмЧРмДЬлКФ `declare_program!`, `to_account_metas`, generic proxy forwarding, nested account-meta builder usageл•Љ м∞ЊмІА л™їнЦИлЛ§.
- м¶Й **dependency signalмЭА м°імЮђнХШмІАлІМ current exploit pathлКФ лѓЄнЩХмЭЄ** мЭілЛ§.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД MicrostableмЭі router/adapter/plugin/proxy instructionмЭілВШ generated-client based forwardingмЭД лПДмЮЕнХШл©і A117мЭА м¶ЙмЛЬ нЩЬмД±нЩФлР† мИШ мЮИлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A117 Anchor Composite AccountMeta Signer-Override Drop / Privilege Downgrade Bypass | explicit signer-downgrade request (`to_account_metas(Some(false))`) fails to propagate through composite/nested account serialization, preserving unexpected signer bits on forwarded metas | proxy/adapter privilege leak, impossible-after-downgrade branches become reachable, external CPI authority misuse despite apparent signer stripping | repo is on Anchor `0.31.1`, but current code scan found no direct meta-forwarding path; **NOT ACTIVE today**, future router/adapter/proxy features must regression-test serialized signer bits |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-20 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A117** added after reclassifying Anchor `#3322` from an adjacent signal into an independent privilege-downgrade-bypass vector. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM active finding** in this cycle; the new pattern is future-facing unless meta-forwarding/proxy flows are introduced.

## 2026-04-22 Anchor Account-Parser Collision Pattern Addition

### D52. Anchor Composite Account-Group Name Collision / Instruction Parser Ambiguity Smuggle

**Source signals (2026-04-22 sweep)**:
- `solana-foundation/anchor` commit `df44381` / PR `#4401` (`Fix name collision in composite account de-duplicator`, 2026-04-21)
- upstream description: composite de-duplicator compared newly collected composite groups only against prior composites, not against top-level instruction account entries, allowing duplicate generated names or duplicate account-group definitions in final parser input

**Key insight**: нМАмЭА л≥інЖµ "IDL/к≥Дм†Х л©ФнГАлКФ нФДл†ИмЮДмЫМнБђк∞А мГЭмД±нЦИмЬЉлЛИ лВілґА мЭЉкіАмД±кєМмІА л≥імЮ•лРЬлЛ§" к≥† к∞Ам†ХнХЬлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ мЭі л≤ДкЈЄлКФ **мЬДм°∞лРЬ metadataл•Љ м£ЉмЮЕнХШмІА мХКмХДлПД**, honest-generated output лВілґАмЧРмДЬ composite account groupк≥Љ vanilla instruction accountк∞А к∞ЩмЭА мЭіл¶ДмЭілВШ к∞ЩмЭА layoutл°Ь мґ©лПМнХі **instruction parser / generated clientк∞А мХ†лІ§нХЬ м†ХмЭШл•Љ мЖМлєДнХШлПДл°Э** лІМлУ§ мИШ мЮИмЭМмЭД л≥імЧђм§АлЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. attacker-controlled repo change, compromised contributor, or unsafe upstream dependency introduces a composite account shape whose generated name/layout collides with a normal instruction account entry that appears later in the emitted output.
2. vulnerable de-duplicator keeps both definitions because it only checked composite-vs-composite collisions, not composite-vs-top-level instruction entries.
3. downstream parser, generated client, validator helper, or policy engine reuses the final output and resolves the duplicate name/group ambiguously (first-wins, last-wins, merge, or silent overwrite).
4. operator or integration code believes it is validating/signing one account schema, while the effective parsed structure maps to another group with different account ordering, mutability, or semantic meaning.
5. runtime program logic may stay unchanged, yet off-chain admission, signing, simulation, or policy enforcement is steered onto the wrong trust boundary.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D31 / A112** = metadata source authenticity or trust-boundary confusionмЭілЛ§.
- **D52** = sourceлКФ honest-generated artifactмЭЉ мИШ мЮИмІАлІМ, **artifact лВілґАмЭШ duplicate account-group identity** к∞А parser ambiguityл•Љ лІМлУ†лЛ§.
- **A117** = signer privilege downgrade serialization failureлЛ§.
- **D52** = privilege bit мЮРм≤ік∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ, **instruction/account-schema selection ambiguity** к∞А нХµмЛђмЭілЛ§.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. к∞РмВђмЮРлКФ лМАк∞Ь on-chain Rust logicмЩА IDL м°імЮђ мЧђлґАлКФ л≥імІАлІМ, generated account-group namespace collisionкєМмІАлКФ мЮШ л≥імІА мХКлКФлЛ§.
2. duplicate nameмЭі мГЭк≤®лПД buildк∞А м¶ЙмЛЬ кє®мІАмІА мХКмЬЉл©і, parser consumerл≥Д first-wins/last-wins м∞®мЭік∞А лТ§лК¶к≤М лђЄм†Ьл•Љ лУЬлЯђлВЄлЛ§.
3. нМАмЭА "нФДл†ИмЮДмЫМнБђк∞А лІМлУ† мґЬл†•" мЭілЭЉлКФ мЭімЬ†л°Ь мВ∞мґЬлђЉ лВілґА мЭЉкіАмД± к≤Ам¶ЭмЭД мГЭлЮµнХШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.
4. exploitмЭА лЯ∞нГАмЮД мљФлУЬ л≥Ам°∞к∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ **client/parser/policy plane мШ§лПД** л°Ь лВШнГАлВШмДЬ, мВђк≥† нЫДмЧРлПД root causeл•Љ business logicмЧРмДЬлІМ м∞ЊкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.

**PoC scenario (future Anchor parser/client migration)**:
```rust
// generated parser output contains two account groups with effectively colliding identity
// downstream tooling resolves one implicitly
let parsed = instruction_parser.parse(ix_data, emitted_accounts)?;

// operator believes `parsed.accounts.authority` came from the canonical top-level schema
// but parser actually bound the colliding composite definition
sign_or_validate(parsed)?;
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- Anchor `#4401` fix нПђнХ® л≤Дм†ДмЬЉл°Ь мШђл¶і к≤Г
- generated IDL / parser input / account-group metadataмЧРмДЬ **duplicate group name, duplicate layout alias, ambiguous instruction-account mapping** мЭД CIмЧРмДЬ мЛ§нМ® м≤Шл¶ђнХ† к≤Г
- parser consumerлІИлЛ§ first-wins/last-wins/overwrite semanticsл•Љ нЕМмК§нКЄл°Ь к≥†м†ХнХ† к≤Г
- "generated by framework" л•Љ лђімШ§л•Ш к∞Ам†ХмЬЉл°Ь мВЉмІА лІРк≥†, emitted account-group namespace diffл•Љ л¶іл¶ђмК§ мХДнЛ∞нМ©нКЄ к≤Ам¶Э нХ≠л™©мЧР лД£мЭД к≤Г
- migration мЛЬ old/new parser к≤∞к≥Љл•Љ к∞ЩмЭА instruction corpusмЧР лМАнХі diff нХШмЧђ account ordering, mutability, signer expectationмЭі л∞ФлАМмІА мХКмХШлКФмІА нЩХмЭЄнХ† к≤Г

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/package.json` мЭА `@coral-xyz/anchor = ^0.31.1` мЭік≥†, нЕМмК§нКЄлКФ `target/types/microstable` + `anchor.workspace.microstable` л•Љ мВђмЪ©нХЬлЛ§.
- нШДмЮђ л†ИнПђмЧРмДЬлКФ Anchor 1.0 к≥ДмЧімЭШ new instruction parser / composite account-group parser migration нЭФм†БмЭД м∞ЊмІА л™їнЦИлЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД Anchor parser/client generation migrationмЭілВШ richer composite account schema лПДмЮЕ мЛЬ, generated artifact diffмЩА duplicate-group lintк∞А нХДмИШлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D52 Anchor Composite Account-Group Name Collision / Instruction Parser Ambiguity Smuggle | composite-account de-duplicator fails to compare composite groups against top-level instruction account entries, leaving duplicate generated names or duplicate group definitions in parser input | ambiguous client/parser binding, wrong account-schema validation, signing/policy checks applied to unintended account ordering or semantics | repo is still on Anchor `0.31.1` and current code scan found no Anchor 1.0 parser migration path; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but future parser/client upgrades must lint duplicate group identities and diff generated schemas |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-22 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D52** added from Anchor `#4401` after classifying it as a parser-plane ambiguity vector rather than a mere metadata hygiene bug. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM active finding** in this cycle; the new pattern is future-facing unless parser/client migration is introduced.

## 2026-04-24 zkVM Guest-Parser Forgery Pattern Addition

### A118. zkVM Guest Unchecked Deserialization / Enum Jump-Table Proof Forgery

**Source signals (2026-04-24 sweep)**:
- Trail of Bits, `We beat GoogleвАЩs zero-knowledge proof of quantum cryptanalysis` (2026-04-17)
- Google patch reference in updated paper / disclosure trail

**Key insight**: лІОмЭА нМАмЭі zk л≥імХИ м†Рк≤АмЭД нХ† лХМ verifying key, public input binding, ceremony transcript к∞ЩмЭА **мХФнШЄнХЩм†Б verifier к≤љк≥Д** лІМ л≥ЄлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ мЭі мВђл°АлКФ verifierмЩА verification keyк∞А кЈЄлМАл°ЬмЧђлПД, **proofл•Љ лІМлУЬлКФ guest program / simulator / parser м™љмЧРмДЬ unsafe deserializationк≥Љ enum dispatch confusionмЭі мЮИмЬЉл©і лПЩмЭЉ verification key мХДлЮШмДЬлПД forged proofк∞А лІМлУ§мЦімІИ мИШ мЮИмЭМ** мЭД л≥імЧђм§АлЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol or research system accepts attacker-controlled private bytes that are meant to deserialize into guest-program instructions / operation vectors.
2. guest code uses unchecked deserialization (`access_unchecked`, unsafe casts, unchecked archive access, or equivalent) because proof cost or runtime cost makes validation look expensive.
3. malformed bytes inject an out-of-range enum / opcode / variant tag that survives into the simulator or prover execution path.
4. compiled jump table / dispatch logic takes an unintended branch, so accounting, bounds, or semantic checks are skipped while the execution result still looks acceptable enough to satisfy the final statement.
5. prover emits a proof that verifies under the **same verification key** as the honest proof, so downstream verifiers cannot distinguish forged execution from a legitimate optimization.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A49 / A87** = trusted setup or verifier-constant collapseлЛ§.
- **A50** = Fiat-Shamir transcriptмЧРмДЬ public claim bindingмЭі лє†мІД нМ®нДімЭілЛ§.
- **A118** = verifier mathлВШ VK driftк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ, **guest/prover implementation itself** к∞А malformed private bytesл•Љ мЮШл™ї л∞ЫмХДлУ§мЧђ proofл•Љ мЬДм°∞нХШлКФ нМ®нДімЭілЛ§.
- м¶Й A118мЭШ нХµмЛђмЭА **same-VK proof forgery via unsafe guest parsing / dispatch confusion** мЭілЛ§.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. лІОмЭА к∞РмВђ л≤ФмЬДк∞А verifier contract / proof system spec / setup ceremonyмЧР мєШмЪ∞м≥Р мЮИк≥†, proving guestлВШ simulator мљФлУЬлКФ мД±лК• мљФлУЬл°Ь мЈ®кЄЙлРШмЦі мЦХк≤М л≥ЄлЛ§.
2. unsafe deserializationмЭА "proof generation cost м†Ик∞Р" мЭілЭЉлКФ л™ЕлґДмЬЉл°Ь нХ©л¶ђнЩФлРШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.
3. enum discriminant / jump-table UBлКФ мЦЄмЦі мИШм§А undefined behaviorмЩА proving semanticsк∞А к≤єм≥Р мЮђнШДмЭі мЦіл†µлЛ§.
4. мВђк≥† нЫДмЧРлПД нМАмЭА "verification keyлПД к∞Щк≥† verifierлПД мХИ л∞ФлАМмЧИлКФлН∞ мЩЬ мЬДм°∞к∞А лРШмІА" лЭЉлКФ мЛђл¶ђм†Б нХ®м†ХмЧР лє†мІАкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE: attacker-controlled private bytes trusted as a valid archived structure
let private_bytes = zkvm::io::read_vec();
let ops = unsafe {
    rkyv::access_unchecked::<rkyv::Archived<Vec<Op>>>(&private_bytes)
};

for op in ops.iter() {
    match op.kind {
        OpKind::CriticalGate => stats.critical += 1,
        OpKind::CheapGate => stats.cheap += 1,
        _ => {}
    }

    // second dispatch can jump to unintended branch when enum tag is invalid
    execute(op);
}

// SAFER: validate untrusted bytes before guest execution, reject invalid enum tags,
// and keep counting / execution over the same checked representation
let private_bytes = zkvm::io::read_vec();
let ops = rkyv::from_bytes::<Vec<Op>>(&private_bytes)?;
for op in &ops {
    op.validate()?;
    execute_checked(op, &mut stats)?;
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- proving guest / simulatorлПД verifierмЩА к∞ЩмЭА мИШм§АмЭШ л≥імХИ к∞РмВђ л≤ФмЬДмЧР нПђнХ®нХ† к≤Г
- private witness bytesмЧР лМАнХі unchecked deserializationмЭД кЄИмІАнХШк±∞лВШ, мµЬмЖМнХЬ enum tag / length / pointer-offset validationмЭД л≥ДлПД мД†нЦЙнХ† к≤Г
- cost accountingк≥Љ semantic executionмЭД мДЬл°Ь лЛ§л•Є unchecked dispatchмЧР лВШлИДмІА лІРк≥†, **validated IR** нХЬ л≤Мл°Ь к≥µмЬ†нХ† к≤Г
- лПЩмЭЉ verification key мХДлЮШмДЬ forged proofк∞А к∞АлК•нХЬмІА л≥ілКФ negative testл•Љ лСШ к≤Г (false low-cost / false bounded-resource / false state-transition proof)
- `unsafe` л•Љ proof-cost м†Ик∞Р мВђмЬ†л°Ь мВђмЪ©нХ† к≤љмЪ∞, UBк∞А proof statementл°Ь м†ДмЭілРШмІА мХКлКФмІА л≥ДлПД м¶Эл™Е лШРлКФ adversarial fuzzл•Љ мЪФкµђнХ† к≤Г

**Microstable relevance**:
- нШДмЮђ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` мЩА `keeper/src/` мЧРлКФ SP1/RISC0/Groth16 guest verifierлВШ zkVM proving pathк∞А мЧЖлЛ§.
- `keeper/src/hermes.rs` мЭШ `Proof::WormholeMerkle` лКФ accumulator / merkle proof м≤Шл¶ђл°Ь, мЭіл≤И A118мЭШ zkVM guest-forgery нМ®нДік≥ЉлКФ лЛ§л•ілЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД proof-backed reserve attestations, zk oracle attestations, zk coprocessor, privacy-preserving solvency proofsл•Љ лґЩмЭіл©і A118мЭА м¶ЙмЛЬ relevant нХімІДлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A118 zkVM Guest Unchecked Deserialization / Enum Jump-Table Proof Forgery | attacker-controlled private bytes are deserialized without validation inside a zkVM guest/prover; invalid enum/opcode tags trigger unintended dispatch so accounting or semantic checks are skipped, yet the emitted proof still verifies under the same verification key | same-VK forged proof, false resource bounds, false execution attestations, proof-backed mint/oracle/reserve fraud without VK drift | current Microstable code has no zkVM proving path; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future zk-backed attestation / proof coprocessor design must audit guest parser safety as a first-class trust boundary |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-24 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A118** added after classifying the Trail of Bits same-VK forged-proof result as a guest/prover implementation vector, not a verifier-key or transcript-binding bug. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM active finding** in this cycle; the new pattern is future-facing unless zk-backed proving components are introduced.

## 2026-04-28 Immutable Legacy Package Pattern Addition

### A119. Immutable Legacy Package / Shared-State Version-Gate Bypass

**Source signals (2026-04-28 sweep)**:
- Scallop / sSUI rewards incident coverage (incident 2026-04-26, public mechanism write-ups 2026-04-27)

**Key insight**: лІОмЭА нМАмЭі мГИ UI, мГИ SDK, мГИ package/versionмЬЉл°Ь мВђмЪ©мЮРл•Љ мШЃкЄ∞л©і мШИм†Д к≤љл°ЬлКФ мВђмЛ§мГБ м£љмЧИлЛ§к≥† лКРлВАлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ **мШИм†Д л∞∞нПђл≥ЄмЭі м≤імЭЄ мЬДмЧР кЈЄлМАл°Ь лВ®к≥†, к∞ЩмЭА shared object / shared state / rewards pool / vault authorityл•Љ к≥ДмЖН лІМмІИ мИШ мЮИлЛ§л©і, deprecated pathлКФ мЧђм†ДнЮИ live privileged code** лЛ§. мЭіл≤И Scallop мВђл°АмЭШ нХµмЛђмЭА лЛ®мИЬнХЬ `last_index` міИкЄ∞нЩФ л≤ДкЈЄлІМмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ, **кЈЄ л≤ДкЈЄк∞А лВ®мХД мЮИлКФ мШЫ packageк∞А мЧђм†ДнЮИ нШДмЮђ rewards stateмЧР мУ∞кЄ∞ кґМнХЬмЭД к∞АмІД м±Д нШЄмґЬ к∞АлК•нЦИлЛ§** лКФ м†РмЭілЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol migrates users to a newer package / contract / module and assumes routing through the new path is sufficient retirement.
2. old package remains immutable and callable on-chain.
3. shared objects / shared pools / shared PDAs / shared mint authority are not version-gated to the current package only.
4. attacker calls the deprecated path directly, bypassing current SDK/UI restrictions.
5. latent bug in the old path (uninitialized accumulator/index, weaker auth, stale invariant, outdated math) mutates live shared state or extracts current rewards/assets.

**2026-05 reinforcement (Renegade + Huma + Transit + Aurellion)**:
- **Renegade V1 (2026-05-10)**: legacy Arbitrum deploymentк∞А faulty migrationмЬЉл°Ь version counterк∞А мЦікЄЛлВЬ м±Д лВ®мХД мЮИмЧИк≥†, unprotected initializerк∞А лЛ§мЛЬ мЧіл¶ђл©імДЬ deprecated V1 мЮРм≤ік∞А live drain pathк∞А лРРлЛ§.
- **Huma Finance V1 (2026-05-11)**: нМАмЭА мЭілѓЄ V2л°Ь лДШмЦік∞Ак≥† V1мЭД sunset м§СмЭімЧИмІАлІМ, deprecated BaseCreditPoolмЧР лВ®мЭА protocol/pool-owner fee balanceлКФ к≥ДмЖН value-bearing surfaceмШАлЛ§.
- **Transit Finance legacy TRON contract (2026-05-13)**: нШДмЮђ к≥ДмХљмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **2022-era deprecated contract** к∞А л≥ДлПДл°Ь мВімХД мЮИмЧИк≥†, кЈЄ мЮФмЧђ к≤љл°ЬлІМмЬЉл°ЬлПД $1.88MмЭі лє†м†ЄлВШк∞ФлЛ§.
- **Aurellion Labs Diamond Proxy (2026-05-12)**: re-initialization мЮРм≤ілКФ нШДмЮђ кґМнХЬ нГИмЈ® л≤°нД∞мШАмІАлІМ, мЛ§м†Ь мВђмЪ©мЮР мЖРмЛ§мЭА **мШИм†ДмЧР лВ®мХД мЮИлНШ ERC-20 approvals** к∞А к≥ДмЖН live authorityмШАкЄ∞ лХМлђЄмЧР л∞ЬмГЭнЦИлЛ§.

к≥µнЖµм†РмЭА `old surface is no longer the intended path` мЩА `old surface can no longer move value` к∞А лЛ§л•ілЛ§лКФ м†РмЭілЛ§. м¶Й A119лКФ immutable packageлІМмЭШ нКємИШ мВђл°Ак∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ, **legacy execution path + residual authority persistence** мЭЉл∞Ш кµђм°∞мЭШ лМАнСЬ кЄ∞мИ† л≤°нД∞лЛ§.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A10 Logic Bug** лКФ нКєм†Х нХ®мИШ лВілґА к≤∞нХ® мЮРм≤іл•Љ лІРнХЬлЛ§. **A119** лКФ кЈЄ к≤∞нХ®мЭі **retired лРШмЧИмЦімХЉ нХ† legacy execution pathк∞А мХДмІБ live authorityл•Љ мЬ†мІА** нХШкЄ∞ лХМлђЄмЧР мЛ§м†Ь мЖРмЛ§л°Ь мЭімЦімІАлКФ кµђм°∞л•Љ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§.
- **B69 Upgrade Asynchrony** лКФ нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬ AмЩА мЩЄлґА нММнКЄлДИ/нЖµнХ© к≤љк≥ДмЭШ лєДлПЩкЄ∞ мЧЕкЈЄл†ИмЭілУЬ м±ЕмЮД к≥µл∞±мЭілЛ§. **A119** лКФ **к∞ЩмЭА нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬ лВілґАмЭШ кµђл≤Дм†Д package / sidecar / module** мЭі shared stateл•Љ к≥ДмЖН к±ілУЬл¶і мИШ мЮИлКФ кµђм°∞лЛ§.
- **A119мЭШ нХµмЛђмЭА stale code мЮРм≤іл≥ілЛ§ stale codeмЭШ кґМнХЬ мІАмЖНмД±** мЭілЛ§.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. к∞РмВђ л≤ФмЬДк∞А current package / current entrypoint / current SDKмЧР мІСм§СлРШк≥†, к≥Љк±∞ л∞∞нПђл≥ЄмЭА "unused" лЭЉлКФ мЪімШБ к∞Ам†ХмЬЉл°Ь л∞Ал†§лВЬлЛ§.
2. immutable-chain нЩШк≤љмЧРмДЬлКФ old codeл•Љ мІАмЪ∞мІА л™їнХШмІАлІМ, many teams mentally treat UI migration as decommission.
3. shared object or shared-state кґМнХЬмЭі version field мЧЖмЭі лВ®мХД мЮИмЦілПД happy-path testingмЭА мµЬмЛ† к≤љл°ЬлІМ нГАлѓАл°Ь лЖУмєШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.
4. мВђк≥† нЫДмЧРлПД нМАмЭА "core contracts are safe" лЭЉк≥† лІРнХШкЄ∞ мЙђмЫМ, мЛ§м†Ь privileged legacy surfaceк∞А лК¶к≤М мЮђлґДл•ШлРЬлЛ§.

**Code pattern to find**:
```move
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: deprecated package can still mutate live shared state.
public entry fun claim_legacy_rewards(pool: &mut RewardsPool, user: &mut UserSpool) {
    // MISSING: assert!(pool.active_package == CURRENT_PACKAGE_ID, E_DEPRECATED_PATH);
    // MISSING: assert!(user.last_index_initialized, E_UNINITIALIZED_INDEX);

    let delta = pool.spool_index - user.last_index;
    let reward = delta * user.staked_amount;
    transfer_rewards(pool, reward);
}

// SAFER SHAPE: bind shared state to the active version/package and reject retired paths.
public entry fun claim_rewards(pool: &mut RewardsPool, user: &mut UserSpool) {
    assert!(pool.active_package == CURRENT_PACKAGE_ID, E_DEPRECATED_PATH);
    assert!(user.last_index_initialized, E_UNINITIALIZED_INDEX);
    ...
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- migration нЫДмЧРлПД old package / old program / old sidecarк∞А live shared stateмЧР м†СкЈЉ к∞АлК•нХЬмІА л≥ДлПД inventoryл•Љ лСШ к≤Г
- shared object / PDA / vault authorityмЧР **active version / active program binding** мЭД лСРк≥†, retired pathлКФ hard rejectнХ† к≤Г
- "SDK no longer routes there" л•Љ retirement м¶Эк±∞л°Ь мЈ®кЄЙнХШмІА лІР к≤Г
- мЭім†Д л∞∞нПђл≥ЄмЧР latent bugк∞А мЮИмЦілПД current stateл•Љ л™ї лІМмІАк≤М **authority migration / state migration / access revocation** мЭД нХ®кїШ мЩДл£МнХ† к≤Г
- audit checklistмЧР **historical live entrypoint reachability** л•Љ л≥ДлПД нХ≠л™©мЬЉл°Ь мґФк∞АнХ† к≤Г

**Microstable relevance**:
- нШДмЮђ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` лКФ лЛ®мЭЉ `declare_id!` кЄ∞л∞Ш мЧЕкЈЄл†ИмЭілУЬнШХ Solana program к≤љл°Ьл•Љ мВђмЪ©нХЬлЛ§.
- repo scan кЄ∞м§А, нШДмЮђ мљФлУЬл≤†мЭімК§мЧРлКФ **parallel retired program ID**, legacy rewards sidecar, or old module that still writes the same live shared state under a different on-chain package pathк∞А нЩХмЭЄлРШмІА мХКмХШлЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД rewards / bridge / attestation / auxiliary programмЭД **мГИ program IDл°Ь лґДл¶ђ лІИмЭікЈЄл†ИмЭімЕШ** нХШл©імДЬ shared PDA/vault authorityл•Љ мЮђмВђмЪ©нХШл©і, A119лКФ м¶ЙмЛЬ мЮђнПЙк∞А лМАмГБмЭі лРЬлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A119 Immutable Legacy Package / Shared-State Version-Gate Bypass | deprecated immutable package/program remains callable and still has authority over current shared state, so an old latent bug can drain live rewards/assets despite current SDK/UI using a newer path | legacy-path drain, reward inflation, stale-auth bypass, post-migration false sense of safety | current Microstable scan shows a single upgradeable Solana program path and no parallel retired write-capable package; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future multi-program migration must bind shared PDAs/vaults to the active version |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-28 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A119** added after Scallop made the mechanism public enough to separate **legacy-package reachability + shared-state authority persistence** from a generic logic bug. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A119 itself**; the open **B45 audit-attestation gap** remains the top current HIGH continuity issue.

## 2026-04-29 Multi-Hop Route Minimum Aggregation Pattern Addition

### A120. Multi-Hop Route Minimum Aggregation / Terminal-Settlement Mismatch

**Source signals (2026-04-29 sweep)**:
- Rhea Finance / Burrowland postmortem coverage (incident 2026-04-16, fuller public mechanism synthesized by 2026-04-28)
- Burrowland source links referenced in the postmortem:
  - `margin_trading.rs#get_token_out()` `#L101-L113`
  - `margin_position.rs#is_min_amount_out_reasonable()` `#L268-L456`
  - `margin_trading.rs#on_open_trade_return()` `#L328-L398`

**Key insight**: лІОмЭА нФДл°ЬнЖ†мљЬмЭі multi-hop swap routeмЭШ `min_amount_out` л•Љ "мµЬмҐЕ мВ∞мґЬ л≥імЮ•к∞Т"м≤ШлЯЉ лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ route parserк∞А **м§Ск∞Д hopмЭШ minimumкєМмІА л™®лСР мµЬмҐЕ outputм≤ШлЯЉ нХ©мВ∞** нХШк±∞лВШ, execution callbackмЧРмДЬ **мЛ§м†Ь terminal outputмЭі кЈЄ minimumмЭД лІМм°±нЦИлКФмІА лЛ§мЛЬ к≤Ам¶ЭнХШмІА мХКмЬЉл©і**, slippage/health checksлКФ м°імЮђнХілПД м†ДлґА к∞АмІЬ мХИм†ДмЮ•мєШк∞А лРЬлЛ§. мЭіл≤И Rhea мВђл°АмЭШ нХµмЛђмЭА к∞АмІЬ мЮРмВ∞лІМмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ, **route semanticsл•Љ мЮШл™ї мЭљмЭА admission logic + settlement-time recheck лґАмЮђ** мШАлЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. attacker creates or controls a multi-hop route whose intermediate asset appears repeatedly.
2. route parser derives the "minimum expected output" by summing `min_amount_out` values from each hop instead of isolating the true terminal output.
3. protocol compares that inflated figure against endpoint-asset oracle checks and declares the route reasonable.
4. DEX executes faithfully and returns only a tiny real terminal amount.
5. settlement callback credits whatever arrived, but does **not** recheck `actual_terminal_output >= validated_minimum` and does not recompute health/solvency before finalizing success.
6. attacker opens an undercollateralized position or receives real assets against effectively worthless output, then liquidation / reserve depletion magnifies losses.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A98** лКФ fake asset / attacker-created marketк∞А oracle or admission layerл•Љ мЖНмЭілКФ лђЄм†ЬлЛ§. **A120** мЭА мЮРмВ∞мЭі лђімЧЗмЭілУ† мГБкіАмЧЖмЭі, **multi-hop route semanticsмЩА final settlement accountingмЭі мЦікЄЛлВШлКФ к≤Г мЮРм≤і** к∞А нХµмЛђмЭілЛ§.
- **B35 / A63** лКФ slippage parameterк∞А 0мЭік±∞лВШ мІАлВШмєШк≤М лКРмК®нХЬ misconfigurationмЭД лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. **A120** мЭА slippage checksк∞А м°імЮђнХілПД **лђімЧЗмЭД minimumмЬЉл°Ь к≥ДмВ∞нЦИлКФмІА** к∞А нЛАл¶ђл©і кЈЄлМАл°Ь лђіл†•нЩФлРЬлЛ§лКФ м†РмЭі лЛ§л•ілЛ§.
- **A10 Logic Bug** л≥ілЛ§ лНФ мҐБк≥† мЮђнШД к∞АлК•нХЬ нМ®нДімЭілЛ§. нХµмЛђ primitiveлКФ **intermediate-hop minimum aggregation + post-execution terminal-settlement mismatch** лЛ§.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. happy-path testsлКФ л≥інЖµ single-hop лШРлКФ м†ХмГБ multi-hop routeлІМ к≤Ам¶ЭнХШк≥†, **к∞ЩмЭА intermediate assetмЭі л∞Шл≥µлРШлКФ route** л•Љ adversarialнХШк≤М лІМлУ§мІА мХКлКФлЛ§.
2. admission checkмЩА execution callbackк∞А мДЬл°Ь лЛ§л•Є нХ®мИШ/л™®лУИмЧР лВШлЙШмЦі мЮИмЦі, auditorк∞А "validated before swap" мЩА "settled after swap" мВђмЭімЭШ semantic continuityл•Љ лЖУмєШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.
3. oracle reasonableness checkк∞А мЮИмЬЉл©і нМАмЭі кЈЄ мЬДмЧР к≥ЉмЛ†нХШкЄ∞ мЙљмІАлІМ, **мЮЕл†•лРЬ minimum мЮРм≤ік∞А inflated** лРШмЦі мЮИмЬЉл©і oracle checkлКФ мХИм†ДмЭД м¶Эл™ЕнХШмІА л™їнХЬлЛ§.
4. post-swap callbackлКФ мҐЕмҐЕ "success/failure bookkeeping" мЬЉл°Ь мЈ®кЄЙлРШмЦі, мЛ§м†Ь output-vs-promised-output мЮђк≤Ам¶ЭмЭі лє†м†ЄлПД severityк∞А лК¶к≤М лУЬлЯђлВЬлЛ§.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: sums intermediate minima and never rechecks terminal output.
fn parse_route_minimum(route: &[SwapAction]) -> u128 {
    route.iter().map(|step| step.min_amount_out as u128).sum()
}

fn on_swap_return(actual_out: u128, validated_minimum: u128) {
    // MISSING: require!(actual_out >= validated_minimum);
    credit_position(actual_out);
    finalize_success();
}

// SAFER SHAPE: derive terminal-only minimum and recheck after execution.
fn parse_terminal_minimum(route: &[SwapAction]) -> u128 {
    route.last().map(|step| step.min_amount_out as u128).unwrap_or(0)
}

fn on_swap_return(actual_out: u128, validated_minimum: u128) {
    require!(actual_out >= validated_minimum, Error::SettlementShortfall);
    recompute_health_factor()?;
    finalize_success();
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- route parserлКФ intermediate-hop minimaл•Љ нХ©мВ∞нХШмІА лІРк≥† **terminal asset кЄ∞м§А** мЬЉл°Ь normalize нХ† к≤Г
- route validation нЫД execution callbackмЧРмДЬ **actual output vs validated minimum** мЭД л∞ШлУЬмЛЬ мЮђк≤Ам¶ЭнХ† к≤Г
- swap мЩДл£М нЫД position open / borrow finalize м†ДмЧР **health factor / collateral sufficiency** л•Љ лЛ§мЛЬ к≥ДмВ∞нХ† к≤Г
- multi-hop route fuzzingмЧР repeated intermediate asset, fake pools, and late-settlement shortfall casesл•Љ нПђнХ®нХ† к≤Г
- attacker-created pools м∞®лЛ®мЭА мЧђм†ДнЮИ м§СмЪФнХШмІАлІМ, **trusted poolsлІМмЬЉл°ЬлПД A120мЭА л∞ЬмГЭнХ† мИШ мЮИмЭМ** мЭД л≥ДлПД мЭЄмЛЭнХ† к≤Г

**Sources**: https://rekt.news/rhea-finance-rekt | https://github.com/ref-finance/burrowland/blob/2110c7047f2d72a9189abcbaad5eafb4e4a87f9e/contracts/contract/src/margin_trading.rs#L101-L113 | https://github.com/ref-finance/burrowland/blob/2110c7047f2d72a9189abcbaad5eafb4e4a87f9e/contracts/contract/src/margin_position.rs#L268-L456 | https://github.com/ref-finance/burrowland/blob/2110c7047f2d72a9189abcbaad5eafb4e4a87f9e/contracts/contract/src/margin_trading.rs#L328-L398

**Microstable relevance**:
- нШДмЮђ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` мЭШ `rebalance()` лКФ DEX route, multi-hop swap, callback settlementл•Љ мИШнЦЙнХШмІА мХКк≥† **weight parameter update only** л•Љ м≤Шл¶ђнХЬлЛ§.
- `keeper/src/rebalance.rs` мЩА `keeper/src/wire.rs` лПД `ix_rebalance(..., max_slippage_bps, ...)` л°Ь on-chain weight update instructionлІМ м†ДмЖ°нХШл©∞, route path / `min_amount_out` / DEX aggregator calldataл•Љ мЛ£мІА мХКлКФлЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД keeperк∞А Jupiter/Orca/Raydium multi-hop swapмЭД мІБм†С м°∞л¶љнХШк±∞лВШ margin-like delayed settlement flowл•Љ лґЩмЭіл©і A120мЭА м¶ЙмЛЬ мЮђнПЙк∞А лМАмГБмЭілЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A120 Multi-Hop Route Minimum Aggregation / Terminal-Settlement Mismatch | protocol mis-parses multi-hop route minima by counting intermediate hops as terminal guarantees, then finalizes execution without rechecking actual terminal output against the validated minimum | undercollateralized position open, borrow against worthless output, reserve depletion, liquidation cascade despite nominal slippage/oracle checks | current Microstable code has no multi-hop route parser, no DEX callback settlement path, and `rebalance` only updates weights; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future swap-integrated keeper path must treat this as first-class |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-29 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A120** added after the fuller Rhea/Burrowland mechanism showed a reusable exploit class beyond generic fake-asset admission. Matrix is now **127+ named vectors + META-01~61 + B73~B78 = 188+ total entries**. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A120 itself**; **B45 audit-attestation gap** remains the top current HIGH continuity issue.

## 2026-05-01 Fixed-Polynomial Opening Omission Pattern Addition

### A121. Fixed-Polynomial Evaluation Opening Omission / Same-VK Proof Forgery

**Source signals (2026-05-01 KST sweep)**:
- OtterSec, `Unverified Evaluations in Dusk's PLONK` (published 2026-04-30)
- Dusk / Rusk public code links referenced in the write-up

**Key insight**: лІОмЭА нМАмЭі zk verifierл•Љ л≥Љ лХМ transcript binding, setup ceremony, public input hashingкєМмІАлІМ л≥ЄлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ PLONKл•Ш мЛЬмК§нЕЬмЧРмДЬлКФ verifierк∞А мµЬмҐЕ л∞©м†ХмЛЭмЧР лД£лКФ к∞Б scalarк∞А л∞ШлУЬмЛЬ (a) verifierк∞А мІБм†С к≥ДмВ∞нЦИк±∞лВШ, (b) мЭілѓЄ committed polynomialмЧР лМАнХі **opening proofл°Ь лђґмШАк±∞лВШ**, (c) verifier key commitmentл•Љ мІБм†С мВђмЪ©нХімХЉ нХЬлЛ§. Dusk мВђл°АмЭШ нХµмЛђмЭА proverк∞А **fixed/public selector polynomialмЭШ evaluation к∞Т** мЭД proof structмЧР мЛ§мЦі л≥ілГИк≥†, verifierк∞А кЈЄк≤ГмЭД final equationмЧР мВђмЪ©нХШл©імДЬлПД **кЈЄ к∞ТмЭі verifier-key commitmentмЩА мЭЉмєШнХШлКФмІА м†ДнША мЧімЦіл≥імІА мХКмХШлЛ§** лКФ м†РмЭілЛ§. кЈЄ мИЬк∞Д нХілЛє scalarлКФ transcriptмЧР лђґмЭЄ к∞ТмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **attacker-controlled free variable** л°Ь лПМмХДмШ®лЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol uses a PLONK-like verifier with fixed/public polynomials (selectors, lookup tables, verifier-key-backed polynomials) committed during preprocessing.
2. prover includes claimed evaluations of those fixed polynomials at challenge point `zeta` inside the proof payload.
3. verifier uses those scalars in linearization / quotient / final pairing equations.
4. however, verifier never checks an opening proof tying those evaluations back to the trusted commitments in the verifier key.
5. attacker chooses evaluation values that make the final equation pass, forging a proof for a false statement under the **same verification key**.
6. downstream application accepts a syntactically valid proof and releases value, admits invalid state transitions, or validates forged privacy actions.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A49** лКФ trusted setup / verifying key constants мЮРм≤ік∞А мЮШл™їлРЬ к≤љмЪ∞лЛ§. **A121** мЭА setup constantsк∞А м†ХмГБмЭілНФлЭЉлПД, verifierк∞А **proof мХИмЭШ evaluation scalarл•Љ commitment-bound valueл°Ь к≤Ам¶ЭнХШмІА мХКлКФ** кµђнШД к≤∞нХ®мЭілЛ§.
- **A50** мЭА Fiat-Shamir transcriptмЧР public claimмЭі м†ЬлХМ нЭ°мИШлРШмІА мХКмХД statementк∞А free variableмЭі лРШлКФ нМ®нДімЭілЛ§. **A121** мЭА transcript orderingмЭі лІЮмХДлПД, **opening proofк∞А лє†мІД fixed-polynomial evaluation** мЭі free variableмЭі лРЬлЛ§лКФ м†РмЭі лЛ§л•ілЛ§.
- **A118** мЭА guest/prover parserмЭШ unsafe deserializationл°Ь same-VK forgeryк∞А мГЭкЄ∞лКФ нМ®нДімЭілЛ§. **A121** мЭА proverк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ **verifier-side commitment-binding omission** мЭілЛ§.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. лІОмЭА к∞РмВђк∞А witness polynomial openingмЭА кЉЉкЉЉнЮИ л≥імІАлІМ, selector / fixed polynomialмЭА "мЦім∞®нФЉ public circuit data" лЭЉк≥† л≥ік≥† prover-supplied evaluationмЭД лНЬ мЭШмЛђнХЬлЛ§.
2. verifier codeк∞А linearization / MSM / pairing checkл°Ь кЄЄмЦімІАл©і, мЦілЦ§ scalarк∞А locally derivedмЭЄмІА, мЦілЦ§ scalarк∞А opening-boundмЭЄмІА мґФм†БнХШкЄ∞к∞А кЄЙк≤©нЮИ мЦіл†§мЫМмІДлЛ§.
3. transcript bindingмЭі лІЮлКФмІАлІМ нЩХмЭЄнХШк≥†, **final equationмЧР лУ§мЦік∞АлКФ л™®лУ† мЩЄлґА scalarк∞А мЛ§м†Ь commitment-boundмЭЄмІА** кєМмІАлКФ м≤інБђл¶ђмК§нКЄмЧР мЧЖлКФ к≤љмЪ∞к∞А лІОлЛ§.
4. implementationмЭі gnark к∞ЩмЭА мХИм†ДнХЬ кµђм°∞л•Љ "лєДмКЈнХШк≤М" лФ∞л•ілКФ к≤Гм≤ШлЯЉ л≥імЧђлПД, custom widget / batched opening мµЬм†БнЩФк∞А лУ§мЦік∞Ал©і мЭі к≤љк≥Дк∞А лђілДИмІАкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE: verifier consumes prover-supplied fixed/selectors evaluations
// without opening them against verifier-key commitments.
let q_l_eval = proof.q_l_eval;
let q_r_eval = proof.q_r_eval;
let q_m_eval = proof.q_m_eval;
let q_o_eval = proof.q_o_eval;

let gate_term = q_m_eval * a_eval * b_eval
    + q_l_eval * a_eval
    + q_r_eval * b_eval
    + q_o_eval * c_eval;

verify_pairing(gate_term, witness_openings, quotient_opening)?; // MISSING: open(q_*, zeta)

// SAFER: every prover-supplied fixed/public evaluation must be commitment-bound.
verify_opening(vk.q_l_commitment, zeta, proof.q_l_eval, proof.q_l_opening)?;
verify_opening(vk.q_r_commitment, zeta, proof.q_r_eval, proof.q_r_opening)?;
verify_opening(vk.q_m_commitment, zeta, proof.q_m_eval, proof.q_m_opening)?;
verify_opening(vk.q_o_commitment, zeta, proof.q_o_eval, proof.q_o_opening)?;
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- final verifier equationмЧР лУ§мЦік∞АлКФ prover-supplied scalarл•Љ м†ДлґА лґДл•ШнХі `local / opening-bound / commitment-direct` мЕЛ м§С нХШлВШк∞А мХДлЛИл©і м¶ЙмЛЬ к≤љк≥†нХ† к≤Г
- fixed/selectors/lookup-table evaluations лПД witness evaluations мЩА лПЩмЭЉнХЬ мИШм§АмЬЉл°Ь opening-proof к≤Ам¶ЭмЭД мЪФкµђнХ† к≤Г
- custom widget / batched opening / linearization optimizationмЭД лД£мЭД лХМ **"proofмЧРмДЬ мЭљмЧИмІАлІМ мЧімЦіл≥імІА мХКмЭА field"** diffл•Љ CIмЧРмДЬ к≤АмВђнХ† к≤Г
- same verification key мХДлЮШмДЬ false statement proofл•Љ мЛЬлПДнХШлКФ negative testл•Љ лСШ к≤Г
- verifier л¶ђлЈ∞мЧРмДЬ "public polynomialмЭілЛИ мХИм†Д" мЭілЭЉлКФ мІБкіАмЭД кЄИмІАнХШк≥†, мЛ§м†Ь binding pathл•Љ нСЬл°Ь мґФм†БнХ† к≤Г

**Sources**: https://osec.io/blog/2026-04-30-unverified-evaluations-dusk-plonk/ | https://github.com/dusk-network/plonk | https://github.com/dusk-network/rusk

**Microstable relevance**:
- нШДмЮђ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` мЩА `keeper/src/` мЧРлКФ PLONK/Groth16/STARK verifier, proof-backed reserve attestation, zk coprocessor, or same-VK proof acceptance pathк∞А мЧЖлЛ§.
- `keeper/src/hermes.rs` мЭШ `Proof::WormholeMerkle` лКФ Wormhole/Pyth accumulator-merkle verification к≤љк≥Дл°Ь, мЭіл≤И A121мЭШ **fixed-polynomial opening omission** к≥ЉлКФ лЛ§л•Є нБілЮШмК§лЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД zk-backed collateral attestations, proof-based solvency, privacy-preserving reserve statementsл•Љ лґЩмЭіл©і A121мЭА м¶ЙмЛЬ relevant нХімІДлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A121 Fixed-Polynomial Evaluation Opening Omission / Same-VK Proof Forgery | verifier consumes prover-supplied evaluations of selector/fixed/public polynomials in the final proof equation without opening them against trusted commitments, turning those scalars into attacker-controlled free variables under the same verification key | forged proof for false statements, unbacked mint/withdraw, invalid shielded transfer admission, value release despite intact setup/transcript ordering | current Microstable code has no zk verifier or proof-acceptance path; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future zk-attestation integration must audit commitment-binding completeness as a first-class boundary |

**Matrix state as of 2026-05-01 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A121** added after the Dusk PLONK disclosure separated **verifier-side opening omission** from setup failures (A49), transcript-binding failures (A50), and guest-parser forgeries (A118). Matrix is now **128+ named vectors + META-01~63 + B73~B78 = 191+ total entries**. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A121 itself**; current result is future-facing only.

## 2026-05-14 Anchor Zero-Copy Validation Opt-Out Pattern Addition

### A122. Anchor Zero-Copy Validation Opt-Out / AccountLoader Trust Collapse

**Source signals (2026-05-14 KST sweep)**:
- Anchor commit `9d452e3` (`feat: allow bypassing owner/disc checks on zero copy accounts (#4162)`, merged 2026-05-13)
- Anchor PR `#4162` title history explicitly broadened from bypassing owner/discriminator checks to a supported API surface

**Key insight**: лІОмЭА Solana нМАмЭі `AccountLoader<T>` л•Љ л≥іл©і вАЬzero-copyлЭЉмДЬ лє†л•імІАлІМ Anchor accountмЩА лєДмКЈнХШк≤М мХИм†ДнХШлЛ§вАЭлКФ мЭЄмГБмЭД л∞ЫлКФлЛ§. кЈЄлЯ∞лН∞ мЭіл≤И upstream changeлКФ initialized account pathмЧРмДЬлПД **owner/discriminator к≤Ам¶ЭмЭД мЪ∞нЪМнХШлКФ к≥µмЛЭ мІДмЮЕм†Р** мЭД лНФ лЕЄмґЬнЦИлЛ§. мЭі мИЬк∞Д мЬДнЧШмЭА лЛ®мИЬнХЬ `unsafe` мВђмЪ©мЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ, **нФДл†ИмЮДмЫМнБђк∞А нЧИмЪ©нХЬ convenience APIк∞А к≤Ам¶Э мЩДл£М к∞Эм≤ім≤ШлЯЉ л≥імЭікЄ∞ мЛЬмЮСнХЬлЛ§** лКФ лН∞ мЮИлЛ§. л¶ђлЈ∞мЦік∞А вАЬмЭілѓЄ Anchor loader нГАмЮЕмЭілЛИкєМ кіЬм∞ЃлЛ§вАЭк≥† мІАлВШк∞Ал©і, мЛ§м†Ьл°ЬлКФ arbitrary same-layout bytesл•Љ privileged stateм≤ШлЯЉ мЭљк±∞лВШ мУ∞лКФ trust collapseк∞А мГЭкЄілЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol introduces a zero-copy account path for hot state (`vault`, `config`, `oracle cache`, `strategy state`, `position sidecar`, etc.).
2. developer reaches for `AccountLoader::new_unchecked` or a similar owner/discriminator-bypass helper because the account is вАЬalready constrained elsewhereвАЭ, is part of a migration path, or is sourced from CPI / sidecar plumbing.
3. one call-site later, code performs `load()` / `load_mut()` and treats the returned struct as trusted initialized state.
4. attacker supplies an account that matches expected byte layout or passes a weaker outer constraint, but is not actually the intended typed account instance.
5. business logic now runs on forged/stale/aliased state: authority fields, limits, debt, weights, oracle metadata, or replay guards are read from attacker-controlled bytes.
6. result can be state confusion, policy bypass, stale-state replay, or mutation of the wrong logical object without any explicit signature failure.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A96** лКФ duplicate mutable aliasingмЭілЛ§. **A122** лКФ aliasingмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **typed-state validation мЮРм≤іл•Љ framework-level opt-outмЬЉл°Ь лђілДИлЬ®л¶ђлКФ нМ®нДі** мЭілЛ§.
- **A112** лКФ raw IDL / metadata trust-boundary confusionмЭілЛ§. **A122** лКФ IDLмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **runtime account loading path** мЭШ owner/discriminator invariantsк∞А лђілДИмІАлКФ лђЄм†ЬлЛ§.
- кЄ∞м°і generic вАЬaccount owner/discriminator check лИДлЭљвАЭ лГДмГИмЩАлПД лЛ§л•ілЛ§. **A122мЭШ мГИ мЪФмЖМлКФ к≥µмЛЭ zero-copy loader APIк∞А opt-outмЭД м†ХлЛєнЩФнХШл©імДЬ мљФлУЬл¶ђлЈ∞ к≤љк≥Дк∞А мХљнХімІДлЛ§** лКФ м†РмЭілЛ§.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. `AccountLoader<T>` мЮРм≤ік∞А typed wrapperлЭЉмДЬ raw `UncheckedAccount` л≥ілЛ§ нЫ®мФђ мХИм†ДнХі л≥імЭЄлЛ§.
2. migration / CPI / plugin sidecar / performance optimization лђЄлІ•мЧРмДЬ вАЬмЧђкЄ∞мДЬлКФ мЭілѓЄ к≤Ам¶ЭнЦИлЛ§вАЭ лКФ м£ЉмДЭмЭі мЙљк≤М нХ©л¶ђнЩФлРЬлЛ§.
3. zero-copy мљФлУЬлКФ bytes вЖФ struct к≤љк≥Дк∞А мІІмХД л≥імЧђлПД, мЛ§м†Ь trust boundaryлКФ **лИДк∞А owner/discriminatorл•Љ л≥ім¶ЭнЦИлКФк∞А** мЧР лЛђл†§ мЮИлЛ§.
4. framework upstream changeк∞А feature нШХнГЬл°Ь лУ§мЦімШ§л©і, нМАмЭА кЈЄк≤ГмЭД л≥імХИ мЩДнЩФк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ м†ХмГБ нЩХмЮ•мЬЉл°Ь л∞ЫмХДлУ§мЭікЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: initialized account path uses unchecked zero-copy loader
// and later treats the bytes as fully validated state.
let loader = AccountLoader::<VaultState>::new_unchecked(vault_info);
let mut vault = loader.load_mut()?;
require!(vault.status == STATUS_ACTIVE, ErrorCode::VaultPaused);
vault.total_debt = vault.total_debt.checked_add(delta).unwrap();

// SAFER SHAPE: initialized state must keep owner + discriminator binding.
let loader = AccountLoader::<VaultState>::try_from(vault_info)?;
let mut vault = loader.load_mut()?;

// If an unchecked path is truly unavoidable, constrain it to creation-only
// or one-shot migration code and re-establish explicit owner/PDA/type checks.
require_keys_eq!(*vault_info.owner, program_id, ErrorCode::InvalidOwner);
require_keys_eq!(vault_info.key(), expected_pda, ErrorCode::InvalidPda);
assert_disc(vault_info, VaultState::DISCRIMINATOR)?;
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- initialized zero-copy stateмЧРмДЬлКФ `AccountLoader::try_from` л•Љ кЄ∞л≥Єк∞ТмЬЉл°Ь к≥†м†ХнХШк≥†, `new_unchecked` / `try_from_unchecked` лКФ **creation-only or one-shot migration** к≤љл°Ьл°Ь нХЬм†ХнХ† к≤Г
- unchecked loader мВђмЪ© мЛЬ owner / PDA / discriminator / version fieldл•Љ к∞ЩмЭА нХ®мИШ мХИмЧРмДЬ мЮђк≤Ам¶ЭнХШк≥†, кЈЄ к≤Ам¶ЭмЭі лБЭлВШкЄ∞ м†ДмЧРлКФ business fieldл•Љ мЭљмІА лІР к≤Г
- code reviewмЧРмДЬ вАЬtyped wrapperлЛИкєМ мХИм†ДвАЭ мґФл°†мЭД кЄИмІАнХШк≥†, zero-copy loader call-siteлІИлЛ§ **who established owner+type invariants?** л•Љ м£ЉмДЭмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ нЕМмК§нКЄл°Ь м¶Эл™ЕнХ† к≤Г
- fuzz / property testмЧР same-layout forged account, stale migrated account, wrong discriminator with valid size, wrong owner with valid bytes мЉАмЭімК§л•Љ мґФк∞АнХ† к≤Г
- performance мµЬм†БнЩФлВШ migration нОЄмЭШ лХМлђЄмЧР unchecked pathл•Љ мГБмЛЬ hot pathл°Ь мКєк≤©нХШмІА лІР к≤Г

**Sources**: https://github.com/solana-foundation/anchor/commit/9d452e3aa450590bafd1e16590952ec64c9d8df7 | https://github.com/solana-foundation/anchor/pull/4162

**Microstable relevance**:
- нШДмЮђ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` мЩА `keeper/src/` мК§мЇФмЧРмДЬ `AccountLoader`, `#[account(zero_copy)]`, `bytemuck`, `new_unchecked`, `try_from_unchecked` мВђмЪ© нЭФм†БмЭА нЩХмЭЄлРШмІА мХКмХШлЛ§.
- мШ®м≤імЭЄ к≤љл°ЬмЭШ `UncheckedAccount` мВђмЪ©м≤Ш(`MigrateLegacyState`, `UpdateOraclePyth`, `DevnetForceReinit`)лКФ zero-copy loaderк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ **мИШлПЩ owner/discriminator/PDA к≤Ам¶Э** мЬЉл°Ь л∞©мЦінХШк≥† мЮИлЛ§.
- keeper мЄ°лПД `wire::decode_account()` мЩА `oracle.rs` мЧРмДЬ discriminator/ownerл•Љ мИШлПЩ к≤Ам¶ЭнХЬлЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД keeper hot path лШРлКФ on-chain state migrationмЭД zero-copy convenience pathл°Ь л∞ФкЊЄл©і A122лКФ м¶ЙмЛЬ relevant нХімІДлЛ§. нКєнЮИ мІАкЄИм≤ШлЯЉ Anchor `0.31.1` мЭД мУ∞лКФ мљФлУЬл≤†мЭімК§к∞А нЦ•нЫД upstream APIл•Љ нЭ°мИШнХ† лХМ вАЬмД±лК• мµЬм†БнЩФ к≤Є refactorвАЭ л°Ь лУ§мЦімШ§л©і review missк∞А лВШкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A122 Anchor Zero-Copy Validation Opt-Out / AccountLoader Trust Collapse | framework-supported unchecked zero-copy loader is used on initialized state, collapsing owner/discriminator/type invariants and turning typed-state reads into attacker-controlled or stale-byte interpretation | state confusion, forged authority/limit reads, stale-state replay, mutation of unintended logical object, policy bypass without explicit signer failure | current Microstable repo shows no `AccountLoader`/zero-copy usage and keeps manual owner/discriminator checks on raw-account paths; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future zero-copy refactor should treat unchecked loaders as a first-class red flag |

**Matrix state as of 2026-05-14 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A122** added after Anchor upstream formally exposed an owner/discriminator validation opt-out for zero-copy loaders. Matrix is now **129+ named vectors + META-01~63 + B73~B78 = 192+ total entries**. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A122 itself**; result is future-facing unless the codebase adopts zero-copy loaders.

## 2026-04-28 Multi-Round Bundle Simulation Pattern Addition

### D54. Multi-Round Transaction Simulation Dependency-Bomb / Bundle-Service Asymmetric DoS

**Source signals (2026-04-28 sweep)**:
- arXiv `2604.21169` (submitted 2026-04-23), *Position Paper: Denial-of-Service Against Multi-Round Transaction Simulation*

**Key insight**: лІОмЭА нМАмЭі block builder / private relay / bundle endpointл•Љ лЛ®мИЬнХЬ "лє†л•Є мВђмД§ mempool" м†ХлПДл°Ь мЈ®кЄЙнХЬлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ bundling serviceлКФ л≥інЖµ **мЧђлЯђ transactionмЭД мИЬм∞® мЛЬлЃђл†ИмЭімЕШнХШл©імДЬ мЭім†Д txк∞А лІМлУ† stateл•Љ лЛ§мЭМ txмЧР л∞ШмШБнХШлКФ multi-round execution engine** мЭілЛ§. мЭілХМ к≥µк≤©мЮРлКФ мЛ§м†Ь мЮРл≥Є мЬДнЧШ мЧЖмЭілПД **state dependencyл•Љ к≥†мЭШл°Ь мЦљмЭА bundle** мЭД лНШм†Є simulatorк∞А round-by-roundл°Ь лєДмЛЉ к≥ДмВ∞мЭД л∞Шл≥µнХШк≤М лІМлУ§ мИШ мЮИлЛ§. нХµмЛђмЭА лД§нКЄмЫМнБђ spamмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ, **bundle semantics мЮРм≤іл•Љ мЭімЪ©нХЬ off-chain simulation-cost asymmetry** лЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. attacker identifies a builder / relay / bundle API that simulates candidate bundles before inclusion.
2. attacker crafts a bundle whose later transactions depend on storage/state mutated by earlier transactions, forcing full sequential simulation rather than cheap stateless validation.
3. bundle is designed so failure appears late, or succeeds only under narrow state combinations, keeping simulator work high while attacker capital at risk stays low.
4. atomic inclusion guarantees and bundle-specific admission rules are abused to avoid ordinary capital or mempool exposure costs.
5. repeated submissions consume builder simulation capacity, degrade searcher QoS, reduce builder revenue, or slow block production / bundle servicing.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D20 Denial of Service** лКФ лМАк∞Ь лД§нКЄмЫМнБђ flood, tx spam, validator/path saturation к∞ЩмЭА broad availability attackмЭД лЛ§л£ђлЛ§. **D54** лКФ **stateful multi-round bundle simulator** лЭЉлКФ лНФ мҐБк≥† мГИл°ЬмЪі off-chain execution surfaceл•Љ к≤®лГ•нХЬлЛ§.
- **C25 MEV Extraction / A91 / A92** лКФ orderingмЭД мЭімЪ©нХі profitмЭД лљСлКФ к≥µк≤©мЭілЛ§. **D54** лКФ ordering edgeк∞А мХДлЛИлЭЉ **admission/simulation cost curve мЮРм≤і** л•Љ к≥µк≤©нХЬлЛ§.
- нХµмЛђмЭА chain congestionмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **builder-side sequential simulation semantics** лЛ§.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. мШ®м≤імЭЄ к∞РмВђлКФ contract correctnessмЧР мІСм§СнХШк≥†, bundle simulator / block-builder pipelineмЭА infra лШРлКФ performance concernмЬЉл°Ь лґДл¶ђлРЬлЛ§.
2. лІОмЭА нМАмЭі private orderflowл•Љ anti-MEV defenseл°Ь лПДмЮЕнХШл©імДЬ, кЈЄ к≤љл°ЬмЭШ **admission fairness / simulation budget** мЭА л≥ДлПД мЬДнШСл™®лНЄлІБнХШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§.
3. л≤ИлУ§ мДЬлєДмК§лКФ к≥µк∞Ь mempoolл≥ілЛ§ нПРмЗДм†БмЭілЭЉ мЮђнШД¬ЈкіАмЄ°мЭі мЦіл†µк≥†, к≥µк≤© cost modelлПД мЭЉл∞Ш gas economicsмЩА лЛ§л•ілЛ§.
4. simulatorлКФ correctness нЕМмК§нКЄлКФ мЮИмЦілПД **adversarial cost asymmetry** нЕМмК§нКЄк∞А к±∞мЭШ мЧЖлЛ§.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: bundle service simulates an attacker-controlled sequence
// with full state carry-over and no strict per-origin simulation budget.
fn evaluate_bundle(bundle: &[Tx]) -> SimResult {
    let mut state = load_head_state();
    for tx in bundle {
        state = simulate_with_state_carry(state, tx)?;
    }
    Ok(commit_candidate(state))
}

// SAFER SHAPE: cap multi-round depth/cost and reject dependency-heavy bundles
// before full sequential execution becomes attacker-amplifiable.
fn evaluate_bundle(bundle: &[Tx], budget: &mut Budget) -> SimResult {
    enforce_bundle_size_limit(bundle)?;
    reject_excessive_state_dependency(bundle)?;
    budget.charge_precheck(bundle)?;
    ...
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- bundle / private-relay pathмЧР **per-origin simulation budget, round cap, dependency-depth cap** мЭД лСШ к≤Г
- мЛ§нМ®к∞А лК¶к≤М л∞ЬмГЭнХ†мИШл°Э лєДмЪ©мЭі мї§мІАлКФ late-fail bundleмЭД л≥ДлПД м†РмИШнЩФнХ† к≤Г
- "private orderflow = safer" к∞Ам†Хк≥Љ л≥Дк∞Ьл°Ь **simulator-plane DoS** л•Љ лПЕл¶љ нХ≠л™©мЬЉл°Ь мЬДнШСл™®лНЄлІБнХ† к≤Г
- precheck лЛ®к≥ДмЧРмДЬ state-carry dependencyк∞А к≥ЉлПДнХЬ bundleмЭД м°∞кЄ∞ к±∞м†ИнХШк≥†, admission costл•Љ attackerмЧРк≤М м†Дк∞АнХ† мЮ•мєШл•Љ лСШ к≤Г
- public RPC fallbackмЭі мЮИлНФлЭЉлПД bundle endpoint overload мЛЬ мЮРлПЩ fail-openмЬЉл°Ь лНФ мХљнХЬ к≤љл°ЬмЧР лЦ®мЦімІАмІА мХКк≤М нХ† к≤Г

**Microstable relevance**:
- нШДмЮђ `programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` л∞П `keeper/src/` мК§мЇФмЧРмДЬ `Jito`, `bundle`, `sendBundle`, `dontfront`, private relay, local multi-round bundle simulator нЭФм†БмЭА нЩХмЭЄлРШмІА мХКмХШлЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД keeper rebalances / oracle writes / hedge legsл•Љ **Jito bundle лШРлКФ private relay** л°Ь л≥ілВікЄ∞ мЛЬмЮСнХШл©і D54лКФ м¶ЙмЛЬ мЮђнПЙк∞АнХімХЉ нХЬлЛ§. нКєнЮИ anti-MEV л™©м†БмЬЉл°Ь private orderflowл•Љ лґЩмЭілКФ мИЬк∞Д, **MEV л∞©мЦі к≤љл°Ьк∞А мГИ DoS control plane** мЬЉл°Ь л∞ФлАФ мИШ мЮИлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D54 Multi-Round Transaction Simulation Dependency-Bomb / Bundle-Service Asymmetric DoS | attacker submits state-dependent bundles that force expensive sequential simulator work while keeping own capital/risk low, degrading bundle-service throughput and builder revenue | private-relay slowdown, searcher QoS degradation, builder revenue loss, delayed bundle execution, fail-open risk into weaker public path | current Microstable repo shows **no Jito/private bundle path today**, so **NOT ACTIVE**; future bundle-based keeper routing must treat simulation-budget abuse as a first-class security invariant |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-28 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D54** added after the new arXiv work made **bundle-service simulation-cost asymmetry** concrete enough to separate from generic DoS. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from D54 itself**; the open **B45 audit-attestation gap** remains the top current HIGH continuity issue.

## 2026-05-06 DNSSEC Closest-Encloser Root-Stall Validation-Loop Pattern Addition

### D55. DNSSEC Closest-Encloser Root-Stall Loop / Cross-Zone Validation OOM

**Source signals (2026-05-06 sweep)**:
- RustSec `RUSTSEC-2026-0118` (`hickory-proto`) and `RUSTSEC-2026-0120` (`hickory-net`) вАФ issued 2026-05-01

**Key insight**: лІОмЭА нМАмЭі DNSSEC validatorл•Љ лґЩмЭіл©і "cache poisoningмЭА м§Дк≥† мХИм†ДнХімІДлЛ§" к≥† мГЭк∞БнХЬлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ мЭіл≤И мЛ†нШЄмЭШ нХµмЛђмЭА **к≤Ам¶ЭмЭД лНФ мЧДк≤©нХШк≤М нХЬ к≤љл°Ь мЮРм≤ік∞А мГИл°ЬмЪі liveness kill-switch** к∞А лР† мИШ мЮИлЛ§лКФ м†РмЭілЛ§. `QNAME` мЭі `SOA owner` мЭШ мЮРмЖРмЭЉ к≤ГмЭілЭЉлКФ к∞Ам†ХмЭі кє®мІД cross-zone мЭСлЛµ нХШлВШлІМмЬЉл°Ь, closest-encloser proof к≤Ам¶Э л£®нФДк∞А **DNS rootмЧРмДЬ л©ИмґШ м±Д к≥ДмЖН `base_name()` к≥Љ hash candidate allocationмЭД л∞Шл≥µ** нХЬлЛ§. м¶Й лђЄм†ЬлКФ лЛ®мИЬ parser panicмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ, **proof-validation state machineмЭі trust-boundary мЬДл∞ШмЭД мҐЕл£М м°∞к±і мЧЖмЭі нЭ°мИШ** нХЬлЛ§лКФ лН∞ мЮИлЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. victim keeper / bridge watcher / oracle fetcher uses a Hickory DNSSEC-validating path (`DnssecDnsHandle`) for endpoint resolution.
2. attacker can return or influence a NoData / NXDomain response requiring NSEC3 closest-encloser proof, often via insecure CNAME crossing into a DNSSEC-signed zone.
3. authority section carries an SOA whose owner is **not an ancestor** of the queried name.
4. validator walks `QNAME -> parent -> ... -> root`, but the termination condition is `current == soa_name`; because `soa_name` is never reached, the loop stalls at root.
5. debug builds panic at the root assertion; release builds allocate candidate names / hashes unbounded until memory exhaustion, stalling or killing resolution for downstream RPC / oracle traffic.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D20** = generic resource-exhaustion DoS.
- **D55** = a **DNSSEC proof-validation loop** whose stopping condition silently assumes zone ancestry and therefore becomes attacker-triggerable through cross-zone response semantics.
- **D53** = resolver cache-poisoning via wrong authority-binding context.
- **D55** = no cache poisoning is required; the validator dies while trying to prove non-existence.
- **B58** = QUIC transport-layer panic / liveness loss after name resolution.
- **D55** = resolution itself becomes the kill plane before transport is even reached.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. DNSSEC validation pathлКФ "л≥імХИ к∞ХнЩФ мШµмЕШ" мЬЉл°Ь л≥імЭікЄ∞ мЙђмЫМ availability threat-modelмЭД л≥ДлПДл°Ь мДЄмЪ∞мІА мХКлКФлЛ§.
2. loop bodyк∞А `base_name()` progressionмЭД нХШлѓАл°Ь superficially terminatingнХі л≥імЭімІАлІМ, root лПДлЛђ нЫД invariantк∞А кє®мІИ лХМ release buildмЧРмДЬлКФ к≥ДмЖН нХ†лЛєлІМ л∞Шл≥µнХЬлЛ§.
3. resolver / recursor / clientк∞А к∞ЩмЭА `DnssecDnsHandle` primitiveл•Љ к≥µмЬ†нХі blast radiusк∞А лДУмЭАлН∞, л¶ђлЈ∞лКФ нКєм†Х binary лЛ®мЬДл°ЬлІМ мЭіл§ДмІАкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.
4. мЪімШБмЮРлКФ DNS мЮ•мХ†л•Љ upstream outageл°Ь мШ§мІДнХШкЄ∞ мЙљк≥†, root causeк∞А cross-zone NSEC3 validation pathлЭЉлКФ м†РмЭД лК¶к≤М л≥ЄлЛ§.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: loop terminates only when current candidate == soa owner,
// implicitly assuming soa_name is an ancestor of qname.
let mut name = qname.clone();
while name != soa_name {
    candidates.push(name.clone());
    hashed.push(hash_name(&name)?);
    name = name.base_name(); // stalls at root if soa_name is not an ancestor
}

// SAFER SHAPE: explicitly break on root / non-ancestor condition before
// allocating more candidates.
let mut name = qname.clone();
loop {
    if name == soa_name {
        break;
    }
    if name.is_root() || !qname.zone_of(&soa_name) {
        return Err(Error::InvalidCrossZoneClosestEncloser);
    }
    candidates.push(name.clone());
    hashed.push(hash_name(&name)?);
    name = name.base_name();
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- `hickory-net >= 0.26.1` лШРлКФ лПЩлУ± нМ®мєШ к≤љл°Ьл°Ь мЭінЦЙнХ† к≤Г
- closest-encloser candidate walkмЧР **root break / ancestor proof / allocation cap** мЭД лПЩмЛЬмЧР лСШ к≤Г
- DNSSEC validationмЭД мЉЬлКФ к≤љмЪ∞ insecure CNAME вЖТ signed-zone NoData/NXDomain кµРм∞® к≤љл°Ьл•Љ regression testмЧР лД£мЭД к≤Г
- security-critical RPC/oracle hostname resolutionмЧРмДЬ DNSSEC validator failureл•Љ лЛ®мИЬ retry loopл°Ь лђїмІА лІРк≥† circuit-breaker + fallback policyл°Ь лґДл¶ђнХ† к≤Г
- local validating resolver / sidecarл•Љ лґЩмЭЉ лХМлКФ "validation strictness м¶Эк∞А = availability risk м¶Эк∞А" л•Љ л≥ДлПД SLOл°Ь л™®лНЄлІБнХ† к≤Г

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/Cargo.lock`, `keeper/Cargo.toml`, `keeper/src/price_feed.rs` мК§мЇФмЧРмДЬ `hickory`, `hickory-net`, `hickory-proto`, `trust-dns`, custom DNSSEC validator к≤љл°ЬлКФ нЩХмЭЄлРШмІА мХКмХШлЛ§.
- нШДмЮђ keeperлКФ `reqwest`, `solana-client`, мЛЬмК§нЕЬ DNS нХімДЭ к≤љл°Ьл•Љ мВђмЪ©нХШл©∞, л†ИнПђ лВі local DNSSEC-validating resolver / recursor нЭФм†БлПД л≥імЭімІА мХКмХШлЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД RPC/oracle failover мХЮлЛ®мЧР Rust-native validating resolverлВШ DNSSEC sidecarл•Љ лґЩмЭіл©і D55лКФ м¶ЙмЛЬ relevant нХімІДлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D55 DNSSEC Closest-Encloser Root-Stall Loop / Cross-Zone Validation OOM | DNSSEC validator assumes SOA owner is an ancestor of QNAME during closest-encloser proof construction; cross-zone response stalls at root and allocates unbounded candidates until panic/OOM | endpoint-resolution halt, keeper/oracle liveness loss, debug panic or release OOM, possible fail-open into weaker resolver path | current Microstable dependency scan found no Hickory / DNSSEC validator path, so **NOT ACTIVE today**; future local validating resolver adoption must treat validation-loop availability as a first-class trust boundary |

**Matrix state as of 2026-05-06 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D55** added after classifying `RUSTSEC-2026-0118` / `0120` as a **DNSSEC validation-state-machine liveness failure** distinct from D53 resolver cache poisoning and generic D20 resource DoS. Matrix is now **129+ named vectors + META-01~65 + B73~B78 = 194+ total entries**. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from D55 itself**; current result is future-facing unless a validating resolver layer is introduced.

## 2026-04-26 Recursive DNS Resolver Cache-Poisoning Pattern Addition

### D53. Recursive DNS Sibling-Zone NS Cache Poisoning / Parent-Pool Zone-Context Elevation

**Source signals (2026-04-26 sweep)**:
- RustSec `RUSTSEC-2026-0106` / `GHSA-83hf-93m4-rgwq` (issued 2026-04-22)

**Key insight**: лІОмЭА нМАмЭі keeper / relayer / price fetcher / verifierмЧРмДЬ `https://allowlisted-host` лІМ к∞Хм†ЬнХШл©і DNS лЛ®к≥ДлКФ мЭілѓЄ лБЭлВЬ лђЄм†ЬлЭЉк≥† л≥ЄлЛ§. нХШмІАлІМ мЭіл≤И мЛ†нШЄлКФ лЛ§л•ілЛ§. **мЈ®мХљнХЬ recursive resolverк∞А sibling zoneмЭШ AUTHORITY мДємЕШ NS л†ИмљФлУЬл•Љ parent-zone contextл°Ь нЖµк≥ЉмЛЬнВ§л©і, к≥µк≤©мЮРлКФ лД§нКЄмЫМнБђ к≤љл°Ьл•Љ нГИмЈ®нХШмІА мХКк≥†лПД лЛ§л•Є allowlisted zoneмЭШ authoritative NS cacheл•Љ мШ§мЧЉ** мЛЬнВђ мИШ мЮИлЛ§. м¶Й к≥µк≤©л©імЭА DNS/BGP hijackмЭі мХДлЛИлЭЉ **resolver лВілґАмЭШ query-to-cache trust binding** мЭілЛ§.

**Attack chain**:
1. victim keeper / bot / gateway uses a vulnerable Hickory recursive resolver path for outbound hostname resolution.
2. attacker controls or can answer for a sibling child zone under the same parent delegation boundary.
3. attacker returns an AUTHORITY-section NS record for `victim.poc.` while servicing a lookup for `attacker.poc.`.
4. resolver validates the record against the parent pool zone (`poc.`) instead of the originally queried child zone, so the bailiwick-style check passes and caches `(victim.poc., NS) -> ns.evil.poc.`.
5. subsequent lookups for the victim zone are routed to attacker-controlled authority, enabling false endpoint resolution, service-discovery poisoning, or staged pairing with TLS / verifier weaknesses.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D27** = DNS/BGP or provider-level endpoint takeover that redirects traffic on the network path.
- **D53** = the victim can keep the same hostname, same config, and same upstream allowlist, yet a **recursive resolver cache entry** is poisoned from a sibling-zone response path.
- **A115 / A77** = certificate / revocation verification failures after resolution.
- **D53** = the earlier **authority-discovery cache** is corrupted first, and may later be paired with TLS bugs or misissuance.

**мЩЬ к∞РмВђк∞А лЖУмєШлКФк∞А**:
1. custom recursive resolverлКФ performance / availability componentл°ЬлІМ л≥імЭік≥†, authority-binding boundaryл°Ь мЮШ л™®лНЄлІБлРШмІА мХКлКФлЛ§.
2. bailiwick-style checkк∞А мљФлУЬмЧР м°імЮђнХШл©і, zone contextк∞А мЮШл™їлРЬ м±Д м†БмЪ©лПЉлПД superficially safeнХі л≥імЭЄлЛ§.
3. hostname allowlist, TLS, and RPC quorum reviewк∞А л≥інЖµ лНФ лИИмЧР лЭДмЦі, resolver cache key semanticsлКФ кєКк≤М л≥імІА мХКлКФлЛ§.
4. exploitмЭА registrar takeoverлВШ obvious config diff мЧЖмЭі мЭЉмЦілВШлѓАл°Ь incident triageк∞А upstream DNS compromiseл°ЬлІМ мҐБнШАмІАкЄ∞ мЙљлЛ§.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: cache keys records by their own owner name/type and
// evaluates AUTHORITY records against the parent NS-pool zone, not the
// originating query tuple.
for rr in answer.iter().chain(authority.iter()).chain(additional.iter()) {
    if rr.name().is_subzone(ns_pool.zone()) {
        cache.insert((rr.name().clone(), rr.record_type()), rr.clone());
    }
}

// SAFER SHAPE: cache the full response under the originating query, or enforce
// that AUTHORITY data can only populate cache entries scoped to the query zone.
cache.insert((query.name().clone(), query.record_type()), response.clone());
validate_authority_scope(&response, query.name())?;
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- `hickory-recursor` л•Љ productionмЧР мУ∞мІА лІРк≥†, `hickory-resolver >= 0.26.0` мЭШ response-keyed recursor path лШРлКФ лПЩлУ±нХЬ мИШм†Х к≤љл°Ьл°Ь мЭінЦЙнХ† к≤Г
- security-sensitive RPC / oracle / attestation hostnameмЧР лМАнХі **authoritative NS set drift** л•Љ л≥ДлПД к∞РмЛЬнХ† к≤Г
- local recursive resolverл•Љ мУЄ лХМ sibling-zone poisoning regression testл•Љ лД£мЭД к≤Г
- hostname allowlistл•Љ DNS trust boundary м†Дм≤імЭШ лМАм≤імЮђл°Ь мЈ®кЄЙнХШмІА лІР к≤Г
- failover / multi-RPC мД§к≥ДмЧРмДЬлПД resolver monocultureл•Љ нФЉнХШк≥† independent resolution planeмЭД лСШ к≤Г

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/Cargo.lock` мЩА keeper мЭШм°імД± мК§мЇФмЧРмДЬ `hickory`, `hickory-recursor`, `trust-dns` лКФ нЩХмЭЄлРШмІА мХКмХШлЛ§.
- нШДмЮђ keeperлКФ `reqwest`, `solana-client`, мЛЬмК§нЕЬ нХімДЭ к≤љл°Ьл•Љ мУ∞л©∞, л†ИнПђ лВі custom recursive resolver / DNS sidecar кµђмД± нЭФм†БлПД л≥імЭімІА мХКмХШлЛ§.
- лФ∞лЭЉмДЬ **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лЛ§лІМ нЦ•нЫД л°Ьмїђ recursor, service-discovery sidecar, Rust-native DNS resolution layerл•Љ RPC/oracle failover мХЮлЛ®мЧР лПДмЮЕнХШл©і D53мЭА м¶ЙмЛЬ relevant нХімІДлЛ§.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D53 Recursive DNS Sibling-Zone NS Cache Poisoning / Parent-Pool Zone-Context Elevation | vulnerable recursive resolver caches AUTHORITY-section NS records for a sibling zone under parent-pool context, poisoning authoritative delegation for later lookups | false endpoint resolution, service-discovery poisoning, allowlisted-host trust bypass precursor, potential pairing with TLS/verifier bugs | current Microstable dependency scan found no `hickory` / `trust-dns` path and no custom recursive resolver; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future local recursor or DNS sidecar would make this immediately relevant |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-26 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D53** added after classifying `RUSTSEC-2026-0106` as a resolver-cache authority-binding bug distinct from D27 routing takeover and A115 certificate validation failures. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM active finding** in this cycle; the new pattern is future-facing unless a custom recursive resolver layer is introduced.
